name: Andrew P. McIndoe
subject: Open border extremism
body:
Because I know your time is valuable, and because this issue is vital to national security, I’ll get straight to the point:

| | | [](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zhu88v5pllgascauhviuljpkouu08cfhfkn8akmo_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4c5h2bs)
---
Dear Fellow Conservative,

Because I know your time is valuable, and because this issue is vital to national security, I'll get straight to the point:

I'm writing today to invite you to join other conservatives who are pressuring lawmakers to end Biden's open border extremism by completing a simple 1-minute survey using the link below:

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zvcuivr1lqgr4dda0h2drj1dds1pbcn877kc1ni8_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4c5h2bs)**

It's critical that proactive citizens like you respond to this national poll because despite legacy media's deafening silence on the issue, **this crisis has now reached historic proportions with more than 2 million reported encounters between Border Patrol and illegal immigrants in 2022.**

This is more encounters than ever before in our nation's history--_more than double that of any year during President Donald Trump 's term._

So when people ask me what I think of Biden's immigration policy, I reply: _what immigration policy?_

Beginning the day he took office, Biden's "policies" have created one of the worst humanitarian crises our nation has ever seen.

And now, untold scores of narcotics, violent criminals, and human traffickers are being allowed to flood into American communities, practically unabated, _every single day._

Decades of success advancing conservative policies have taught us that lawmakers in Washington tend to be more responsive when they know that voters are paying attention.

**Will you help us pressure lawmakers to address this urgent matter of national security by completing a brief 1-minute survey on Immigration Reform using the link below?**

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z0m4m8b8hntt4jcb4ik9o5psi3s90fpjfq0cnnpg_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4c5h2bs)**

**Armed with your response, we will work on your behalf to turn up the pressure on lawmakers and compel them to act now.**

Let elected officials know loud and clear that **you _demand_ an end to Biden's open border extremism by completing this quick 1-minute survey using the link below:**

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z62a31pcrqgdp2qp13n84nv5dk77e43gg858jo78_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4c5h2bs)**

Thank you,

Andrew P. McIndoe
VP, Development

**P.S.** We must take action now--this issue cannot wait. [**Thank you for joining the fight by completing the 1-minute survey linked here. >>**](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zv4g1diokils5as6fao44ac19dca533couvbfug8_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4c5h2bs)
---
| | The Heritage Foundation 214 Massachusetts Avenue NE Washington D.C. 20002
---

name: Connor Boyack
subject: Surprising ways your children are taught socialism
body:
Socialism is gaining popularity with our nation’s youth. And it’s no surprise as to why. Politicians like Alexandria Ocasio-

| | | [](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zol8pf8l59s7leutil7b5e0onlklr8qan9omouc8_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4cjh2bs)
---
| | Dear Friend, Socialism is gaining popularity with our nation's youth. And it's no surprise as to why. Politicians like Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Bernie Sanders lure them in with promises of universal health care and a free college education.

But today's young people don't understand the true realities of socialism--or the clever ways it's being introduced to them.

That's why our latest addition to The Libertas Institute's _Tuttle Twins_ series is so important, _["The Tuttle Twins: Subtle Ways Your Kids Are Taught to Embrace Socialism."](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zdqmlprfrj90didq6a6824ivmgb69nm7t4c886ng_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4cjh2bs)_
---

---

[https://act.libertas.org/tt-subtle-ways](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z8643uu8irt48mf8amnqjo1r3hmdcs3gtoclmrjg_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4cjh2bs)

Inside this eBook, you'll get an in-depth look at some of the socialist concepts being fed to your children. But more importantly, you'll discover practical ways to explain socialism while teaching your children important liberty-based principles of how to live free.

If you're concerned with the rise in socialism in our country--especially among our nation's youth--this is the eBook for YOU!

Please use this link to get instant access to your free copy of, [_The Tuttle Twins: Subtle Ways Your Kids Are Taught to Embrace Socialism._](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zn6eki0l81kketr8a13alik77m5ars1selt93peo_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4cjh2bs)

Connor Boyack

| | GWINN, Mich. — Dr. Bob Lorinser, 67, of Marquette, announced the kickoff of his campaign for US Congress on Monday. Lorinser vies for the 2024 Democratic nomination to represent Northern Michigan and the Upper Peninsula in the US House of Representatives. "Congress needs reasonable voices, advocates, and people you can trust," said Lorinser. "To make Democracy successful, we need our leaders to be truth-tellers. They need to be kind, fair, and serve with dignity and integrity. My campaign commits to those values. "I will not rest in my resolve to show Yoopers and Northern Michiganders a better path forward. In the last cycle, our campaign established the groundwork.. We have unfinished business to accomplish.” Lorinser says Representatives take a solemn oath to promote the general well-being of the people they represent. He believes elected officials should promote liberty, equality, and justice for all while facilitating citizens' pursuit of happiness.
"I will champion rural America and its needs," said Lorinser. “My campaign plans to develop a strong field-team throughout the 1st District to get all qualified candidates, at all levels of government, elected after the results of the primaries are determined." The UP family physician and Marquette County Health Department medical director said his team is eager to mobilize a more-active grassroots movement for 2024. He said that his campaign will again focus on issues, not personal attacks or dishonest political mudslinging. "Democrats have a lot of work to do to show fiercely independent rural voters how our policies benefit hardworking families," said Lorinser. "I want this campaign to be an example of how to 'run rural' in a divided Nation. My party must pay attention to districts like ours for long-term success, and our work begins now.
"For the benefit of everyone in Northern Michigan and the UP, we must put the needs of our country before party. To help lower costs and reduce economic burdens for all Americans, we must be the change.
"All residents in Michigan's First — Democrats, Independents, Republicans — need affordable healthcare, housing, education, and childcare," said Lorinser. "We also need infrastructure investment, job opportunities, and economic prosperity. The challenges our rural communities face are some of the most distinct in the Nation, and we need a Congressman eager to listen to constituents and solve real problems."
---

name: Team NPP
subject: The true legacy of Martin Luther King, Jr.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project_
body:
No empty lip service can change that.

[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/57400535/387810636/409185284?refcode=EM_C_230116MLK_N_TEAM_90DO_6_NG_A_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzkwOTIwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImMzNjg4ZjE5LWM2OTUtZWQxMS05ZDdiLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KtwZ9FIr3kYBAoAwygK6PI9zzTlc0srJ1EOYRz-UaWY=)
| Fellow civil rights defender, Today, we honor and commemorate the life of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and the incredible legacy he left behind for us to preserve. You will undoubtedly see plenty of empty lip service in the form of sanitized quotes stripped of their context, from the very people hellbent on disrupting democracy and upending the lifelong work Dr. King and Mrs. Coretta Scott King fought so hard for all to realize.

It is shameful that too many of those making this empty lip service today are, in fact, fighting to uphold the very same systems of oppression he fought against. They would have us believe his work was finished and racism defeated. But we know all too well his work and life were cut far too short, with his fight continuing to this day.

Our youth, immigrants, Black, Indigenous, and other people of color are especially far too familiar with restrictive voter ID laws, intentionally excessive mail-in ballot requirements, voter purges, uncooperative voting hours meant to stifle the working class, excessively long lines, and so much more. Every day we must continue to fight for our democracy. No matter how many “MLK appreciation” tweets, emails, or press releases we see from those responsible for this injustice, as long as they continue to implement and defend more barriers to the ballot box, they will continue to be nothing but lip service.

In true homage to the continued fight, we reaffirm Dr. King’s statement that **“Now is the time to make real the promises of democracy.”** We will not wait. The New Pennsylvania Project will continue to fight for what’s fair and what’s just in honor of Dr. King, Mrs. King and every single person who fought for Civil Rights.

Thank you for your continuous support and efforts toward a democracy that works for everyone. In solidarity,

Team NPP [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/57400536/387810637/409185284?refcode=EM_C_230116MLK_N_TEAM_90DO_6_NG_A_Signoff&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzkwOTIwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImMzNjg4ZjE5LWM2OTUtZWQxMS05ZDdiLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KtwZ9FIr3kYBAoAwygK6PI9zzTlc0srJ1EOYRz-UaWY=) | _The New Pennsylvania Project was founded in May 2021 to expand Pennsylvania’s electorate, so it reflects the Commonwealth’s demographically changing population. In the next four years, NPP will engage and empower young residents and those living in underrepresented immigrant communities and communities of color. We register communities to vote, provide civic education about the issues that matter in their neighborhoods, and mobilize Pennsylvanians to vote in every election._ _[**______**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/57400537/387810638/409185284?refcode=EM_C_230116MLK_N_TEAM_90DO_6_NG_A_BIO&amount=40&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzkwOTIwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImMzNjg4ZjE5LWM2OTUtZWQxMS05ZDdiLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KtwZ9FIr3kYBAoAwygK6PI9zzTlc0srJ1EOYRz-UaWY=)**[DONATE MONTHLY](https://click.everyaction.com/k/57400538/387810639/409185284?refcode=EM_C_230116MLK_N_TEAM_90DO_6_NG_A_BIO&recurring=auto&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzkwOTIwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImMzNjg4ZjE5LWM2OTUtZWQxMS05ZDdiLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=KtwZ9FIr3kYBAoAwygK6PI9zzTlc0srJ1EOYRz-UaWY=)**_

clean_body: No empty lip service can change that. = | Fellow civil rights defender, Today, we honor and commemorate the life of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and the incredible legacy he left behind for us to preserve. You will undoubtedly see plenty of empty lip service in the form of sanitized quotes stripped of their context, from the very people hellbent on disrupting democracy and upending the lifelong work Dr. King and Mrs. Coretta Scott King fought so hard for all to realize. It is shameful that too many of those making this empty lip service today are, in fact, fighting to uphold the very same systems of oppression he fought against. They would have us believe his work was finished and racism defeated. But we know all too well his work and life were cut far too short, with his fight continuing to this day. Our youth, immigrants, Black, Indigenous, and other people of color are especially far too familiar with restrictive voter ID laws, intentionally excessive mail-in ballot requirements, voter purges, uncooperative voting hours meant to stifle the working class, excessively long lines, and so much more. Every day we must continue to fight for our democracy. No matter how many “MLK appreciation” tweets, emails, or press releases we see from those responsible for this injustice, as long as they continue to implement and defend more barriers to the ballot box, they will continue to be nothing but lip service. In true homage to the continued fight, we reaffirm Dr. King’s statement that “Now is the time to make real the promises of democracy.” We will not wait. The New Pennsylvania Project will continue to fight for what’s fair and what’s just in honor of Dr. King, Mrs. King and every single person who fought for Civil Rights. Thank you for your continuous support and efforts toward a democracy that works for everyone. In solidarity, Team NPP = | The New Pennsylvania Project was founded in May 2021 to expand Pennsylvania’s electorate, so it reflects the Commonwealth’s demographically changing population. In the next four years, NPP will engage and empower young residents and those living in underrepresented immigrant communities and communities of color. We register communities to vote, provide civic education about the issues that matter in their neighborhoods, and mobilize Pennsylvanians to vote in every election. = PA ELECTION DATES : Special Elections happening 2/7/2023 for three PA House districts in Allegheny Co only, unless otherwise determined: HD32, HD34, HD35 Municipal Primary: May 16, 2023 Last day to =: May 1, 2023 Last day to =: May 9, 2023 Municipal Election: November 7, 2023 Last day to =: October 23, 2023 Last day to =: October 31, 2023 || || || New Pennsylvania Project PO Box 443 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please. to receive fewer emails from us. to stop receiving any fundraising emails from us. The New Pennsylvania Project is a 501(c)(4). Contributions are not for charitable purposes and are not tax deductible. | Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project __

name: ICYMI News
subject: The greatest armed conflict in human history
body:
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This fact means that we’re lo
This sponsored message from ICYMI News is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z635rket8hueovmfd1bqv7ub7hkgop4r4j21ko60_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4c5h2bs) Dear Fellow American, Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This fact means that we’re losing more and more living memory of the greatest armed conflict in human history, which makes the study of World War II even more important. To this end, Hillsdale College has produced an online course entitled “The Second World Wars.” Taught by historian Victor Davis Hanson, this course explores how the conflict’s diverse theaters, belligerents, and ways of fighting came eventually to define a single war. And it examines the key investments, strategies, and leaders that led the Allied powers to victory over the Axis. [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zve3o7kot0v0togfh7gtj1637a1uh7u4v4b6bn5o_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4c5h2bs) If you’d like to secure a DVD set before they’re all gone, you can get one shipped directly to your home for any gift of $100 or more to Hillsdale College. Here’s a secure link to reserve your copy of “**The Second World Wars** ” on DVD: **[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/support-the-second-world-wars](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zfbhl33p7odgnemi4b80eas63ge1mnj3sbhhdnk0_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ge9pj4c5h2bs)** Thank you for preserving liberty for future generations of Americans through education with Hillsdale College! Best regards, Kyle Murnen, ’09 Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College

name: Andrew P. McIndoe
subject: Open border extremism
body:
Because I know your time is valuable, and because this issue is vital to national security, I’ll get straight to the point: [](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/BC.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c612934%7c7545%7c6%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c1&wdmdk=GVdrIz50rSBNaZwPUjDCHA&sysid=1)

| | | [](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c78607%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=vL%2bWRbfGbSkNs5QqoZ26QA&sysid=1)
---
Dear Fellow Conservative,

Because I know your time is valuable, and because this issue is vital to national security, I’ll get straight to the point:

I’m writing today to invite you to join other conservatives who are pressuring lawmakers to end Biden’s open border extremism by completing a simple 1-minute survey using the link below:

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c78608%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=oGVcEGf6glRxt5umV8wXfw&sysid=1)**

It’s critical that proactive citizens like you respond to this national poll because despite legacy media’s deafening silence on the issue, **this crisis has now reached historic proportions with more than 2 million reported encounters between Border Patrol and illegal immigrants in 2022.**

This is more encounters than ever before in our nation’s history--_more than double that of any year during President Donald Trump’s term._

So when people ask me what I think of Biden’s immigration policy, I reply: _what immigration policy?_

Beginning the day he took office, Biden’s “policies” have created one of the worst humanitarian crises our nation has ever seen.

And now, untold scores of narcotics, violent criminals, and human traffickers are being allowed to flood into American communities, practically unabated, _every single day._

Decades of success advancing conservative policies have taught us that lawmakers in Washington tend to be more responsive when they know that voters are paying attention.

**Will you help us pressure lawmakers to address this urgent matter of national security by completing a brief 1-minute survey on Immigration Reform using the link below?**

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c78608%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=oGVcEGf6glRxt5umV8wXfw&sysid=1)**

**Armed with your response, we will work on your behalf to turn up the pressure on lawmakers and compel them to act now.**

Let elected officials know loud and clear that **you _demand_ an end to Biden’s open border extremism by completing this quick 1-minute survey using the link below:**

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c78608%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=oGVcEGf6glRxt5umV8wXfw&sysid=1)**

Thank you,

Andrew P. McIndoe
VP, Development

**P.S.** We must take action now--this issue cannot wait. [**Thank you for joining the fight by completing the 1-minute survey linked here. >>**](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c78609%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=M4aRMX48gmbgC3%2b2OScKHQ&sysid=1)
---
| | The Heritage Foundation 214 Massachusetts Avenue NE Washington D.C. 20002
---
_107 S West St. #826, Alexandria VA 22314_
_[Unsubscribe](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/OptOut.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c612934%7c7545%7c6%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c1&wdmdk=GVdrIz50rSBNaZwPUjDCHA&sysid=1)_

[](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/CB.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c612934%7c7545%7c6%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c1&wdmdk=GVdrIz50rSBNaZwPUjDCHA&sysid=1)

name: Andrew P. McIndoe
subject: Are you concerned about Biden’s open border extremism?
body:
Because I know your time is valuable, and because this issue is vital to national security, I’ll get straight to the point:

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zva3mgvclcglaoufq1kbhnb1c8m6qtebpmjm6es8_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ie8p32clh2bs)
---
Dear Fellow Conservative,

Because I know your time is valuable, and because this issue is vital to national security, I'll get straight to the point:

I'm writing today to invite you to join other conservatives who are pressuring lawmakers to end Biden's open border extremism by completing a simple 1-minute survey using the link below:

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zj69hvidm9vdpib705n880to9db7nnofh100uv10_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ie8p32clh2bs)**

It's critical that proactive citizens like you respond to this national poll because despite legacy media's deafening silence on the issue, **this crisis has now reached historic proportions with more than 2 million reported encounters between Border Patrol and illegal immigrants in 2022.**

This is more encounters than ever before in our nation's history--_more than double that of any year during President Donald Trump 's term._

So when people ask me what I think of Biden's immigration policy, I reply: _what immigration policy?_

Beginning the day he took office, Biden's "policies" have created one of the worst humanitarian crises our nation has ever seen.

And now, untold scores of narcotics, violent criminals, and human traffickers are being allowed to flood into American communities, practically unabated, _every single day._

Decades of success advancing conservative policies have taught us that lawmakers in Washington tend to be more responsive when they know that voters are paying attention.

**Will you help us pressure lawmakers to address this urgent matter of national security by completing a brief 1-minute survey on Immigration Reform using the link below?**

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zs6c5n9ipo66c0m9q102b4088ciqoq417iauavvg_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ie8p32clh2bs)**

**Armed with your response, we will work on your behalf to turn up the pressure on lawmakers and compel them to act now.**

Let elected officials know loud and clear that **you _demand_ an end to Biden's open border extremism by completing this quick 1-minute survey using the link below:**

**[https://secured.heritage.org/immigration-reform-survey/](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zmtpucu0hb99ibvcck7a46phk60atftdv5r8mucg_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ie8p32clh2bs)**

Thank you,

Andrew P. McIndoe
VP, Development

**P.S.** We must take action now--this issue cannot wait. [**Thank you for joining the fight by completing the 1-minute survey linked here. >>**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z0kl9tp37q6nsm968gj8vacp59elg3kl0e66qpp8_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ie8p32clh2bs)
---
| | The Heritage Foundation 214 Massachusetts Avenue NE Washington D.C. 20002
---

name: Gwen S. Moore
subject: How has Dr. King inspired you?
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Gwen Moore for Congress
body:
My Sisters and Brothers,

Today we commemorate Dr. King’s lifetime of heroism and courageous leadership fighting for equity & equal justice for all.

Each Martin Luther King, Jr. Day, I reflect on how moved I became as I watched his “I Have a Dream” speech as a young girl in Milwaukee. Dr. King inspired me to become a champion and fighter for the people as a Congresswoman. How has he inspired you?

While we have made great progress, there is more to be done. Let us carry on the legacy and mission of Dr. King not only today, but each and every day as we march together toward equality and justice for all!

Together, #WeRepresent!

Gwen
Proudly representing Wisconsin's 4th Congressional District
Proud Co-Leader of the Violence Against Women Act
100% Pro-Choice
100% Democracy Defender

About Gwen: The first African-American woman in Wisconsin history to serve in the U.S. House of Representatives, Congresswoman Gwen Moore, took her official oath of office on January 4, 2005. She served in the Wisconsin State Senate from 1993-2004. Before her election to the Senate, Congresswoman Moore served two consecutive terms in the Wisconsin State Assembly from 1989-92.

name: Anna Paulina Luna
subject: The Left is now attacking me
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANNA PAULINA LUNA FOR CONGRESS
body:
Democrats see the 20 House Conservatives as a threat. [](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/BC.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c210334%7c0461%7c8%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c805&wdmdk=GtfWpQX8soHMYNBAs9KCnQ&sysid=1)

---
Friend,
---
Democrats and the liberal media have been attacking me for being one of the 20 House members who fought for a conservative agenda.
---
**They’ve called me an extremist, a fake Latina, a witch, and a white supremacist.**
---
_But their attacks won’t stop me from voting for secure borders, defunding the 87,000 new IRS agents, and an end to out-of-control spending bills that light your money on fire._
---
This is my first time ever in Congress - or in public office at all!
---
...And it only took a few days for me to become hated by the entire Democrat machine. I can only imagine how much they’ll attack me in the weeks and months to come.
---
[**Please make a contribution to defend me from the wild and outrageous attacks being manufactured by the Democrat machine now that they see me as a threat.**](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c656881%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=H%2bKSWiDX%2fVPp2LJTeD8o7A&sysid=1)

---

As reported by _Fox News_ :
---
[](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c656880%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=dLNqcryq6KDIbUffIXBrog&sysid=1)
---

Our conservative deal has won praise from Tucker Carlson, Laura Ingraham, Steve Bannon, Lou Dobbs, and many more.
---
...And our group of 20 conservative fighters have only just begun to fight for the American people.
---
[](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c656880%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=dLNqcryq6KDIbUffIXBrog&sysid=1)
---

But now, I need your support as we hold the line to ensure that our House majority fights for YOUR priorities.
---
[**So please, make a contribution to support House conservatives in our fight against the destructive Biden-Harris agenda.**](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c656882%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=NuKVjFNRS8Fh%2foUUSnqmdw&sysid=1)

Thank you,

name: Kat Cammack
subject: Remembering Dr. King’s Legacy
body:
| | | | [](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0016BXJ_GKWK1GzMR1CzzkwoJVa-XX0NNVYAl7L1fMSnEtyMP60efIuEzYJvm-KwnUNNUvsoHB4TyrvdNXlWJ2kdbUpupXoWZyH-LxV3F3WhDiEavjyu32Und_28FcUrpcGGV0u9uqPFew5qgkWw5gdTP5xi6FZtW0t&c=HdNpO2jUgcFXdBM_Ym18ckDskfoLhygRjvpAkvf9oz4wtOMlDhykBg==&ch=WsR3LWQ96PbdOfoFv0UGUh9HoqhovPLS-zzMrn2DLeqKJt7QXnJVsA==)
---
| Friend
Today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day, and in honor of one of America's greatest civil rights leaders, I wanted to encourage you to reflect on and remember the legacy Dr. King left for us.
---
| [](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0016BXJ_GKWK1GzMR1CzzkwoJVa-XX0NNVYAl7L1fMSnEtyMP60efIuEzYJvm-KwnUNNUvsoHB4TyrvdNXlWJ2kdbUpupXoWZyH-LxV3F3WhDiEavjyu32Und_28FcUrpcGGV0u9uqPFew5qgkWw5gdTP5xi6FZtW0t&c=HdNpO2jUgcFXdBM_Ym18ckDskfoLhygRjvpAkvf9oz4wtOMlDhykBg==&ch=WsR3LWQ96PbdOfoFv0UGUh9HoqhovPLS-zzMrn2DLeqKJt7QXnJVsA==)
---
| Dr. King stood against some of our nation's most significant divides, and after years of preaching peace and practicing civil disobedience, he inspired a generation of Americans to come together and create a better future for us all.
Martin Luther King left an enormous impact on our nation, and as we honor him today, I hope you can take the time to reflect on his legacy and remember that we are all created equal under God.

name: Nikki Budzinski
subject: Reflecting on his legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nikki For Congress Nikki for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57403756/387815833/-927342243?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5YTQ3MjJjZS1jYjk1LWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EgvCyWOky3JeL_LR3dkR1M0dKEkJT_4ULjpnE7-1SEA=&emci=fd75f4db-8c93-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=9a4722ce-cb95-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=13891387)
| Hi friends,

I wanted to reach out today as we celebrate the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. – an inspiring voice for justice. He dedicated his life to the nonviolent struggle for civil rights in our country and around the world.

Dr. King’s influential writings, powerful speeches, and charismatic leadership shaped this country’s history for the better. We continue learning from his profound legacy, and seek to build on all that he accomplished and sought to achieve in his lifetime.

I hope you join me in taking a moment today to not only acknowledge his memory, but to reflect on how we may honor his legacy in our everyday lives.

Sincerely,

Nikki
---

Paid for by Nikki For Congress Nikki for Congress
PO Box 5171
Springfield, IL 62705
United States

Nikki has spent her life fighting for workers and working families by strengthening unions nationwide, and she was instrumental in passing a $15 minimum wage in Illinois while working as a senior advisor for Governor JB Pritzker. Most recently, Nikki served in President Biden's administration in the office that implemented the American Rescue Plan.

Nikki believes in the American Dream and will bring people together to level the playing field and rebuild the middle class so families have a shot to get ahead.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57403757/387815834/823246156?refcode=em_fr_2022.08.18_XX_all-nd&refcode2=9a4722ce-cb95-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&amount=3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5YTQ3MjJjZS1jYjk1LWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EgvCyWOky3JeL_LR3dkR1M0dKEkJT_4ULjpnE7-1SEA=).

name: American Defense News
subject: Your thoughts on education in America
body:
Are K-12 schools, colleges, and universities in your state doing enough to provide students with a proper understanding of Ameri
| | |
---
| | This sponsored message from American Defense News is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zaq69nbdf1ursehm4eis0oestbaitf94m2rvih7g_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ieco74c5h2bs)
---
| | | Fellow American, Are K-12 schools, colleges, and universities in your state doing enough to provide students with a proper understanding of American history and government--including an understanding of the purposes and principles of the Constitution? There is reason to be doubtful. There seems to be a decreasing sense of patriotism among young Americans, and an increasing attraction to socialism and other ideas destructive of liberty. And have you noticed all the disturbing incidents taking place on college campuses, with students resorting to intimidation, threats, and violence to silence speakers with whom they disagree? Hillsdale is planning to redouble its efforts for the future of liberty. We will respond to these disturbing trends by expanding our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty--especially for younger Americans. And as we form our strategy we'd like to hear from you: [**Please take our brief education survey here » **](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z75iplg1ap36d5del69i0i8is4gviujmq72gpp60_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ieco74c5h2bs) We have done too many of our young people a disservice by failing to provide them with knowledge of our nation's great heritage of liberty. Even worse, many of our schools, corrupted by Left-wing bias, have pushed a false history of America as essentially flawed and unjust. This indoctrination will only continue once students return to campuses across the country. Hillsdale can remain independent and stay true to its mission because it refuses to accept one penny of taxpayer funding--even indirect aid like federal or state student grants and loans. This frees Hillsdale from burdensome and corrupting regulation, but it also means we are entirely dependent on the support of our friends nationwide. Will you help us understand how Americans look at education today so that we can plan our expanded outreach in the smartest way possible? [**Please take a few minutes to complete this survey and let us know your opinions on education today » **](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z4inj7ragfomb039vfd7u4etjihdct6061dnkudo_rp22sh2s8i66pr6c3ieco74c5h2bs) I am deeply grateful for your friendship.
---
Warm Regards,
Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth - Defending Liberty since 1844
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

name: Derek Kilmer
subject: "Darkness cannot drive out darkness, only light can do that."
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
Together, I know we can get closer to living up to our ideals every day.
Peter,

Today, we celebrate the life and legacy of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

As we take time to reflect on Dr. King's contributions to the Civil Rights movement and how far our country has come in the fight for equality, let us also remember that, while Dr. King's dream is still alive, his work remains unfinished.

Dr. King led by example. He showed us when we come together, we have the power to create change. So, while we reflect on the progress we've made in decades past, we also must acknowledge that our work is far from over.

**As Dr. King once said, "Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate; only love can do that."**

The struggle for justice, equality, and peace is ongoing, but together, I know we can get closer to living up to our ideals every day.

I believe in the promises of our nation. A nation where every American can vote without facing discriminatory barriers - where we embrace the principle of equal pay for equal work; where everyone has affordable health care; where every person can live with dignity; and where every person's civil rights are protected and guarded fiercely.

**These are principles that should guide our country, and on this day -- and every day -- I hope you'll join me in recommitting to the fight for a better future for all. **

Martin Luther King Jr. Day is a reminder of what's possible when we refuse to sit quietly in the presence of injustice. I will keep pushing to form that more perfect union, and I am grateful to have you by my side.

It's an honor to serve you,

Derek
---

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/aGsSYNzjUBOZ9RnqHIJ5l-6l11JkLdr4hvq3HL3twbE=/0Yac01hW3qujZollyX2X4n_WPoGTroARHTWlFUV3Rpc=) | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/aGsSYNzjUBOZ9RnqHIJ5l-6l11JkLdr4hvq3HL3twbE=/P8WIXPUH_WFLTJsjd-BK21J59bTkTLyIg2-JJrZDkgI=)
---|---
Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
---

People for Derek Kilmer
PO Box 1381
Tacoma WA 98402 United States

Peter, thank you so much for being a supporter of Derek Kilmer and Democrats up and down the ballot.

Our digital program helps us reach folks with the latest updates. But, if you'd rather not hear from our team, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/aGsSYNzjUBOZ9RnqHIJ5l-6l11JkLdr4hvq3HL3twbE=/aH_2_GhYE33gXc1M6ATnoHPwYsNP9EADLOcNyZ-T1Uc=).

A nation where every American can vote without facing discriminatory barriers - where we embrace the principle of equal pay for equal work; where everyone has affordable health care; where every person can live with dignity; and where every person's civil rights are protected and guarded fiercely. These are principles that should guide our country, and on this day -- and every day -- I hope you'll join me in recommitting to the fight for a better future for all. Martin Luther King Jr. Day is a reminder of what's possible when we refuse to sit quietly in the presence of injustice. I will keep pushing to form that more perfect union, and I am grateful to have you by my side. It's an honor to serve you, Derek = | = ---|--- Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer People for Derek Kilmer PO Box 1381 Tacoma WA 98402 United States Peter, thank you so much for being a supporter of Derek Kilmer and Democrats up and down the ballot. Our digital program helps us reach folks with the latest updates. But, if you'd rather not hear from our team, please =.

name: Jahana Hayes
subject: Dr. King’s legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57403934/387816918/-1295403277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OTRjMWZlOC1jZjk1LWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_gIOaTLsWGGEdv7O6lMzNf3k5il_33nM0eGhYUsBYlE=&emci=2ab992ec-cc95-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=894c1fe8-cf95-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=15529135) | Team, Few leaders stand as tall as Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. in the fight for justice. Today, I hope you and your family reflect on our moment in the ongoing struggle for a just society and commit yourselves to service. In 2023, Dr. King’s fight for justice is still so relevant. Recent development shows that a case that could further shrink the scope of the Voting Rights Act is before a federal appeals court. If successful, it would significantly diminish the legal ability to challenge ballot regulations and redistricting maps that are racially discriminatory. But we also have a lot to be hopeful about. We are still working hard to create a nation where diversity is welcomed and opportunity is abundant. House Democrats welcomed the first Black Gen Z member of Congress. The Congressional Black Caucus saw its largest cohort in 50 years. President Biden and Democrats are continuing to fight for voting rights and civil liberties despite the dysfunction in the GOP. And we have you, friend. Voters and supporters just like you sent a clear message to Washington that you’re done with fear-mongering and policies that don’t work for working people. Voters want to look ahead to what we can build together. And that’s precisely what I plan to do. So thank you again for being one of my top supporters. – Jahana
---

clean_body: | | Team, Few leaders stand as tall as Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. in the fight for justice. Today, I hope you and your family reflect on our moment in the ongoing struggle for a just society and commit yourselves to service. In 2023, Dr. King’s fight for justice is still so relevant. Recent development shows that a case that could further shrink the scope of the Voting Rights Act is before a federal appeals court. If successful, it would significantly diminish the legal ability to challenge ballot regulations and redistricting maps that are racially discriminatory. But we also have a lot to be hopeful about. We are still working hard to create a nation where diversity is welcomed and opportunity is abundant. House Democrats welcomed the first Black Gen Z member of Congress. The Congressional Black Caucus saw its largest cohort in 50 years. President Biden and Democrats are continuing to fight for voting rights and civil liberties despite the dysfunction in the GOP. And we have you, friend. Voters and supporters just like you sent a clear message to Washington that you’re done with fear-mongering and policies that don’t work for working people. Voters want to look ahead to what we can build together. And that’s precisely what I plan to do. So thank you again for being one of my top supporters. – Jahana | | About Jahana** Jahana Hayes proudly represents Connecticut's 5th Congressional district. Keep up with the latest from Jahana on and . ---|--- Email is an important way for us to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. name: Andy Beshear
subject: We continue to follow in Dr. King's footsteps
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANDY BESHEAR FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Let us honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day, Peter.

Today we honor Dr. King and his vision to build a country committed to justice, equality, and fairness for all people. I am proud to connect with you on a day and at a time when social and economic equality, racial justice, and voting rights are at the forefront of the American conscience.

In 1964, 10,000 Kentuckians marched alongside Dr. King in Frankfort, calling for an end to discrimination and segregation. That march helped lead to Kentucky signing the first state civil rights act in the South.

We can learn so much from those years. When we come together and put our differences aside, there is truly nothing we cannot accomplish.

We still have a long ways to go to make Dr. King's dream a reality. But know that I will walk with you — every single Kentuckian, every step of the way — as we continue forward.

Thank you.

Andy

| PAID FOR BY ANDY BESHEAR FOR GOVERNOR
---
Andy Beshear for Governor
PO Box 4278
Louisville, KY 40204
United States

Today, we celebrate **Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., an icon of the Civil Rights Movement and an American hero** who dedicated his life to ensuring every American had the right to vote, regardless of the color of their skin.
**But right now, voter suppression is plaguing our country.**

Over the past two years, **Republicans have introduced over 440 bills in states across the country in an effort to make it harder to vote.**

Let's be clear: These bills target voters of color. And in states like Alabama, Arizona and Florida, they are law.
Republicans have stalled two voting rights bills in the Senate: **the Freedom to Vote Act and t****he John Lewis Voting Rights Act** , which would address racial discrimination impacting the right to vote.
**With a GOP-controlled House and a slim Senate majority, we need to raise our voices and fight like hell to make sure these measures pass****. ****That's why on this MLK Day, we're asking for 5,000 signatures on our petition demanding that lawmakers take action to protect the right to vote. clean_body: | | | | | | Today, we celebrate Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., an icon of the Civil Rights Movement and an American hero who dedicated his life to ensuring every American had the right to vote, regardless of the color of their skin. But right now, voter suppression is plaguing our country. Over the past two years, Republicans have introduced over 440 bills in states across the country in an effort to make it harder to vote. Let's be clear: These bills target voters of color. And in states like Alabama, Arizona and Florida, they are law. Republicans have stalled two voting rights bills in the Senate: the Freedom to Vote Act and the John Lewis Voting Rights Act , which would address racial discrimination impacting the right to vote. With a GOP-controlled House and a slim Senate majority, we need to raise our voices and fight like hell to make sure these measures pass. That's why on this MLK Day, we're asking for 5,000 signatures on our petition demanding that lawmakers take action to protect the right to vote. But we still need 1,582 more. Please, friend, add your name now: | | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. | | Paid for by AB PAC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Connor Boyack
subject: Teaching your children about the dangers of socialism
body:
Socialism is gaining popularity with our nation’s youth. And it’s no surprise as to why. Politicians like Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Bernie Sanders lure them in with promises of universal health care and a free college education. [](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/BC.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c920805%7c7743%7c7%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c0&wdmdk=YJcT%2fd4cU71BLIp1ItEjGA&sysid=1)

| | | [](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c92739%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=mWnSZosmDaYjPi7n2pux%2bw&sysid=1)
---
| | Dear Friend, Socialism is gaining popularity with our nation’s youth. And it’s no surprise as to why. Politicians like Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Bernie Sanders lure them in with promises of universal health care and a free college education.

But today’s young people don’t understand the true realities of socialism--or the clever ways it’s being introduced to them.

That’s why our latest addition to The Libertas Institute's _Tuttle Twins_ series is so important, _[“The Tuttle Twins: Subtle Ways Your Kids Are Taught to Embrace Socialism.”](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c92730%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=RPStlKSAaD%2f2g66VVIB9PA&sysid=1)_
---
| [](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c92731%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=P2WFX9OjTEpsNsL%2b84EO1Q&sysid=1)

Inside this eBook, you’ll get an in-depth look at some of the socialist concepts being fed to your children. But more importantly, you’ll discover practical ways to explain socialism while teaching your children important liberty-based principles of how to live free.

If you’re concerned with the rise in socialism in our country--especially among our nation’s youth--this is the eBook for YOU!

Please use this link to get instant access to your free copy of, [_The Tuttle Twins: Subtle Ways Your Kids Are Taught to Embrace Socialism._](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c92733%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=7uxlvhk8XotWSbHXXHkLnA&sysid=1)

Connor Boyack

name: Joe Shepherd, President of United Rural Democrats
subject: In honor of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr
disclaimer_text: Paid for by United Rural Democrats](https://click
body:
Join us in completing acts of service in honor of Dr. King’s mission | | | Dear Peter, Today marks Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Day. It's a solemn day to remember the great civil rights leader, and continue his tireless work on behalf of marginalized communities across the country. In my hometown, today is also a day of service. Like many communities, we honor this day by contributing our own acts of service to support our neighbors, friends, family, and those who need a helping hand. We come together in honor of Dr. King’s critical work to uplift our most vulnerable. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. was an incredible figurehead for the civil rights movement, and he changed the course of history with his clear, inclusive, and just vision for the future of our country. In rural America especially, his message of leaving no stone unturned and rooting out all injustice still rings true today. All across rural communities, people of color are being turned away from voting booths, LGBTQ+ individuals are losing their rights to love whomever they choose, women are seeing their reproductive rights stripped away, and millions of Americans are struggling with a cost of living crisis and resource deserts. There's no shortage of injustice. But, what I'm exceedingly proud of is the partnerships that we have built with people like you, Peter, to build a system that works for every person. I encourage you to join with us in the fight for civil rights, Peter, as we continue Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.'s work to uplift Black communities and every marginalized group in our society. Thank you for your support. Joe Shepherd, President of URD
---

United Rural Democrats (URD) is leading the effort to rebuild the Democratic Party in rural America. clean_body: Join us in completing acts of service in honor of Dr. King’s mission | | | Dear Peter, Today marks Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Day. It's a solemn day to remember the great civil rights leader, and continue his tireless work on behalf of marginalized communities across the country. In my hometown, today is also a day of service. Like many communities, we honor this day by contributing our own acts of service to support our neighbors, friends, family, and those who need a helping hand. We come together in honor of Dr. King’s critical work to uplift our most vulnerable. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. was an incredible figurehead for the civil rights movement, and he changed the course of history with his clear, inclusive, and just vision for the future of our country. In rural America especially, his message of leaving no stone unturned and rooting out all injustice still rings true today. All across rural communities, people of color are being turned away from voting booths, LGBTQ+ individuals are losing their rights to love whomever they choose, women are seeing their reproductive rights stripped away, and millions of Americans are struggling with a cost of living crisis and resource deserts. There's no shortage of injustice. But, what I'm exceedingly proud of is the partnerships that we have built with people like you, Peter, to build a system that works for every person. I encourage you to join with us in the fight for civil rights, Peter, as we continue Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.'s work to uplift Black communities and every marginalized group in our society. Thank you for your support. Joe Shepherd, President of URD United Rural Democrats (URD) is leading the effort to rebuild the Democratic Party in rural America. If we're going to take back our country, we need a 50-state strategy to organize in every community across the country. Will you support our network of activists on the ground as we work to rebuild our party's infrastructure and flip these areas blue?

name: Brendan Boyle
subject: Honoring a Legacy Through Service and Defending Our Rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Citizens for Boyle
body:

| | Friend, as we observe Martin Luther King, Jr. Day today, we will all reflect on and honor Reverend Doctor King's life and legacy in many different ways.**
** This weekend, my team and I have spent time participating in several service projects taking place in my district across Philadelphia, to lend a hand and thank those who are donating their time and energy in the spirit of community service.

---

---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57386854/387771941/-1785037583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjN2JmZWQ4Zi1hYTk1LWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-PVDj79-PhBQyhH0kUmW82IeR-JdWJ418gSZB6SfYcc=&emci=cec85241-3095-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=c7bfed8f-aa95-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=34470073)
---
| | As we reflect on the impact of Reverend Doctor King's life and work, we acknowledge that we still have a great deal of work to do, not only to realize the possibility of a more equal, tolerant and just society, but to ensure that forces working against these goals do not grow in strength. With this in mind, we owe it not only to Reverend Doctor King's legacy, but to the very future of our country to stand up and defend the rights and institutions that reflect our most cherished values.
When we consider these rights, none is more foundational that the right to vote.
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57386855/387771942/-1785037583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjN2JmZWQ4Zi1hYTk1LWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-PVDj79-PhBQyhH0kUmW82IeR-JdWJ418gSZB6SfYcc=&emci=cec85241-3095-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=c7bfed8f-aa95-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=34470073)
---
Across the United States, we're seeing unrelenting attacks on voting rights, from restrictive access to polling locations, eliminating voting by mail or early voting, and even banning volunteers from providing refreshments to those waiting in line to vote!
**Such efforts are geared toward one goal: to make it harder for all of us to make our voices heard at the ballot box.
** **Those behind these efforts have a vested interest in keeping voters that disagree with them at home on Election Day, because they know when those of us who believe in transparency, free expression and accountability show up and vote, they lose.** **So if we want to make progress on this front, there are things we can do right now: we can pass bills like the Freedom to Vote: John R. Lewis Act, and the John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act, both of which would expand voting rights and protect our votes against attacks that are seen unfolding in state legislatures across the country.** **So my ask to you today is simple:_join me in this fight_.** **As we proceed into a new term in Congress, we will need to keep up pressure on every member in the House and Senate to ensure these bills become law. I am a proud supporter, but to make sure all of my colleagues are with me, I need your help.** **Making calls, writing letters and holding our elected officials accountable on this issue is how we're going to win. And in the spirit of Reverend Doctor King, we must be steadfast in these efforts if we're going to succeed.**
---

---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57386856/387771943/-1785037583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjN2JmZWQ4Zi1hYTk1LWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-PVDj79-PhBQyhH0kUmW82IeR-JdWJ418gSZB6SfYcc=&emci=cec85241-3095-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=c7bfed8f-aa95-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=34470073)
---
**Thanks for standing with us.** **Brendan**
---

name: Hendrell Remus
subject: Holding Infinite Hope
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee Democratic PartyCarol V
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty]

Peter,

"We must accept finite disappointment, but never lose infinite hope."

I am holding fast to these words from Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. today as we honor his towering memory. As a Black American and a native Tennessean, I've felt everything from disappointment to deep anger throughout my life in the face of racism, disenfranchisement, and suppression. In our darkest moments, the fight for justice can seem futile; but it never is.

A truly liberated world is always worth fighting for. Infinite hope is what will get us there.

Hope is more powerful than any act of suppression, and it can never be taken away from us. It's what I strive to carry with me as we continue working to fully realize Dr. King's daring dream of an equitable America. For each of us, achieving that dream starts right here in the state where he gave his life fighting for it.

Today, be reminded that we have the power to protect the most robust democracy in the world and to be a beacon of hope for all nations. But we have to put words into action to get there. Infinite hope gives me strength — through perseverance and solidarity, we can continue working to build a world worthy of Dr. King's memory.

With gratitude and hope,

Hendrell Remus
he/him
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty]

The Tennessee Democratic Party works to elect Democratic candidates who fight for working people. Can you help Take Back Tennessee by giving monthly?

| | | (Already took this action? Forward to friends! Want to unsubscribe? See link below) After carefully weighing the evidence, a bipartisan Congressional committee recommended that Donald Trump be charged with multiple crimes. [**And DOJ Special Counsel Jack Smith should follow that recommendation.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb1a816fa-ba0c-4c1f-88f9-4cbbc2ff5d7c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

Yes, Smith needs to do his own investigation and make his own decision. But the evidence is out there in plain sight, and it is overwhelming. Trump needs to pay for his crimes. And **with Trump and his allies an ongoing threat to our democracy,[ we don't have the luxury to wait forever](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7d1d41f9-d7a4-479e-8dcc-d8d6ed57987b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)** for action. We've already seen the damage House Republicans have done in only two weeks. Just imagine what they'd do if they had the White House too (better yet, just look at what they _already did_).

[**Add your name: Tell Special Counsel Jack Smith to charge Trump for his crimes ASAP**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T090a4a3b-5d16-4ad7-aa55-1e2529c87612/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dasspac-2021-footer?refcode=emailheader&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dasspac-2021-footer?refcode=emailheader&amount=25]
Democrats push for FIRST semi-automatic gun ban in 20 years [https://action.demsofstate.org/a/ban-assault-weapons-8-1-1-1-1]
SIGN YOUR NAME: Demand Congress BAN Assault Weapons → [https://action.demsofstate.org/a/ban-assault-weapons-8-1-1-1-1]
Sign the Petition >> [https://action.demsofstate.org/a/ban-assault-weapons-8-1-1-1-1]
President Biden just sounded the alarm:
https://action.demsofstate.org/a/ban-assault-weapons-8-1-1-1-1 [https://action.demsofstate.org/a/ban-assault-weapons-8-1-1-1-1]He is absolutely right!
For the first time in history, guns are the leading cause of death for kids.
And election workers are wearing bulletproof vests to protect themselves from election conspiracists.
Our nation is in the midst of a gun violence crisis. Every day, more than 110 Americans die from gun violence. We don’t have to be numb to this. We don’t have to live like this. We don’t have to die like this. [https://action.demsofstate.org/a/ban-assault-weapons-8-1-1-1-1]
But without enough public support, Congress may not take this critical action on gun safety. So we’re gathering 15,000 Democrats to pressure Congress to Ban Assault Weapons. The petition closes at midnight. Please sign the petition immediately: [https://action.demsofstate.org/a/ban-assault-weapons-8-1-1-1-1]
Sign the Petition >> [https://action.demsofstate.org/a/ban-assault-weapons-8-1-1-1-1]
Thanks for stepping up,
Team DASS
DASS is a national organization supporting the election of Democratic Secretaries of State who are committed to protecting and preserving voting rights and ballot access for all Americans through fair, honest, open, and accurate elections.
Help us fight back against the GOP's voter suppression agenda by pitching in today!
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dasspac-2021-footer?refcode=footer-button&amount=25]

body:
Meet Jason. He grew up with values that shaped his world view - like hard work, honesty, and fairness. He worked his way through college doing construction. clean_body: Meet Jason. He grew up with values that shaped his world view - like hard work, honesty, and fairness. He worked his way through college doing construction. He later enlisted in the National Guard, then the Army to protect our country, serving three combat tours. **_Last week, Attorney General Miyares announced a civil rights investigation to find out why the administration of Thomas Jefferson High School for Science and Technology failed to notify students who earned national merit recognition._** **_This week, following_**[** _reports_**](http://links.jasonforvirginia.com/u/click?_t=b7c680598d5648c3aaffcf8cea32abf4&_m=72803c8e729a4981bba90bd330481552&_e=MEGqn5bY9AWsXtQaSZXv7zp8V84EZmmjQzSw5VrFCK8P8gTGtutWNQ5j8YeKDR1TXm-CMEyp87PtUEq9HXKxZiTIWEg0JbHPDGGNhy8qwySyeOSB2EYSPYJbqD3ly5hFgSoLbP8SYr42O9jYkxeptDWqqbHe_h5PlPs1obE6fAMnqMMbt0G78-VCBnvIgDESgj6hgbaAkmuSoiH1Z3b07VBMVqJuXdKAtewiMVzPcq5n9ANNbVUNNeV1DAyiUhDRFjd-EZWfJGZRSHQS39oHfkaGTYdITHSbdKcBrTdYx_r2uNZ_8Opsapa5lrDve2jo_-AYKG0GRYOFD6dziyg8GWTRjdZBFlV6CvvrpPp4KNXnYbTfz0-BYdvjm9gLreYW)** _that other high schools in Fairfax County had also withheld merit awards from students, Attorney General Miyares announced that he would expand the civil rights investigation to include all of the Fairfax County Public Schools system._**

_“This session my office is supporting common-sense legislation that supports law enforcement, prioritizes victims, and makes our communities safer. By working with the members of the General Assembly, we hope to crack down on organized retail crime, punish drug dealers peddling lethal fentanyl, support members of the Jewish community, and protect Virginia consumers from policies designed for Californians. I look forward to working with legislators to serve our Commonwealth and bring results for Virginians.”_ **– Attorney General Jason Miyares**

clean_body: | | | | | Last week, Attorney General Miyares announced a civil rights investigation to find out why the administration of Thomas Jefferson High School for Science and Technology failed to notify students who earned national merit recognition. This week, following that other high schools in Fairfax County had also withheld merit awards from students, Attorney General Miyares announced that he would expand the civil rights investigation to include all of the Fairfax County Public Schools system. "Virginia AG Jason Miyares on Fairfax County Schools Probe" “This session my office is supporting common-sense legislation that supports law enforcement, prioritizes victims, and makes our communities safer. By working with the members of the General Assembly, we hope to crack down on organized retail crime, punish drug dealers peddling lethal fentanyl, support members of the Jewish community, and protect Virginia consumers from policies designed for Californians. I look forward to working with legislators to serve our Commonwealth and bring results for Virginians.” – Attorney General Jason Miyares | | In March, the Virginia Employment Commission asked AG Miyares to help prosecute fraudulent claims for unemployment compensation. In response, AG Miyares created the Unemployment Compensation Fraud Unit. These cases are open and ongoing. Promises made, promises kept.** Another repeat, violent offender removed from our streets, and another job well done thanks to AG Miyares’ Major Crimes and Emerging Threats Unit! name: Your America
subject: Your thoughts on education in America
body:
Are K-12 schools, colleges, and universities in your state doing enough to provide students with a proper understanding of American history and governmentincluding an understanding of the purposes and principles of the Constitution? [](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/BC.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c920805%7c7744%7c7%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c0&wdmdk=AMedbOLUPtTeB%2b2qdYF9ZA&sysid=1)
| | |
---
| | This sponsored message from Your America News is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c92746%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=ZgoUkk6A4fOUdTG4MmhePQ&sysid=1)
---
| | | Fellow American, Are K-12 schools, colleges, and universities in your state doing enough to provide students with a proper understanding of American history and government--including an understanding of the purposes and principles of the Constitution? There is reason to be doubtful. There seems to be a decreasing sense of patriotism among young Americans, and an increasing attraction to socialism and other ideas destructive of liberty. And have you noticed all the disturbing incidents taking place on college campuses, with students resorting to intimidation, threats, and violence to silence speakers with whom they disagree? Hillsdale is planning to redouble its efforts for the future of liberty. We will respond to these disturbing trends by expanding our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty--especially for younger Americans. And as we form our strategy we’d like to hear from you: [**Please take our brief education survey here » **](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c92747%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=CNmdOGMZeycObSMGBTMlPw&sysid=1) We have done too many of our young people a disservice by failing to provide them with knowledge of our nation’s great heritage of liberty. Even worse, many of our schools, corrupted by Left-wing bias, have pushed a false history of America as essentially flawed and unjust. This indoctrination will only continue once students return to campuses across the country. Hillsdale can remain independent and stay true to its mission because it refuses to accept one penny of taxpayer funding--even indirect aid like federal or state student grants and loans. This frees Hillsdale from burdensome and corrupting regulation, but it also means we are entirely dependent on the support of our friends nationwide. Will you help us understand how Americans look at education today so that we can plan our expanded outreach in the smartest way possible? [**Please take a few minutes to complete this survey and let us know your opinions on education today » **](http://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c92748%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=cm0YZpgQjpslBPkHJB33KA&sysid=1) I am deeply grateful for your friendship.
---

Warm Regards,
Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth - Defending Liberty since 1844
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

_625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314_

name: State Dems HQ
subject: Can you take a look at what we have on record for you?
body:
Over 7,031 Democrats have stepped up to the plate to become 2023 donors – and their donations are already making a powerful impact. | | |
---
| | We’re already halfway through January, and we’ve got a busy year ahead of us. From New Hampshire and Pennsylvania’s must-win special elections – _in chambers whose control could eventually come down to who wins these special elections!_ – to Virginia’s ultra-competitive elections this fall, we’re gearing up for some tough fights. **To compete, we’re working _now_ to build the infrastructure we need to notch Democratic victories in elections across the country – hiring organizers, making long-term investments, reaching new voters, and so much more. And we’re relying on early support from this grassroots team to help fuel our critical work.** Over **7,031** Democrats have stepped up to the plate to become 2023 donors – and their donations are already making a powerful impact. But we didn’t see your name on our official 2023 donor list. Can you take a look at what we have on record for you? | **Name** | **Email** | **2023 Donor?** | **Suggested Gift**

With you and this grassroots team behind us, we can pave the path to Democratic majorities across the country, building Democratic power seat-by-seat. But it’ll take every Democrat reading this email to take ownership of this fight today – we’re counting on you.

clean_body: Over 7,031 Democrats have stepped up to the plate to become 2023 donors – and their donations are already making a powerful impact. | | | | | We’re already halfway through January, and we’ve got a busy year ahead of us. From New Hampshire and Pennsylvania’s must-win special elections – in chambers whose control could eventually come down to who wins these special elections! – to Virginia’s ultra-competitive elections this fall, we’re gearing up for some tough fights. To compete, we’re working now to build the infrastructure we need to notch Democratic victories in elections across the country – hiring organizers, making long-term investments, reaching new voters, and so much more. And we’re relying on early support from this grassroots team to help fuel our critical work. Over 7,031 Democrats have stepped up to the plate to become 2023 donors – and their donations are already making a powerful impact. But we didn’t see your name on our official 2023 donor list. And through your investment, you’ll have a stake in every state Democratic win this year – knowing that YOU helped lay the foundation. With you and this grassroots team behind us, we can pave the path to Democratic majorities across the country, building Democratic power seat-by-seat. name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Honoring Dr. King’s legacy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF SHERROD BROWN
body:
Sherrod Brown - Senator from Ohio [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87645/images/brown_2018_logo-outlined.png]
You hear me talk a lot about the Dignity of Work, but do you know the origin of that phrase?
It comes from Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., who said, “No work is insignificant. All labor that uplifts humanity has dignity and importance.”
Hard work should pay off for everyone, no matter what kind of work you do -- whether you punch a clock or swipe a badge, earn a salary or make tips, whether you’re raising children or caring for an aging parent, regardless of your race or gender.
Everyone deserves respect, good wages, and a sound financial future.
However, we know all too well that not all work is treated with dignity. Changing that isn’t just a part of our movement -- it’s at the heart of it.
I strive every day to lift up working Ohioans and put them front and center of every policy decision in Congress. That’s why we’ve passed legislation like the CHIPS Act and the Inflation Reduction Act, which will create thousands of good-paying jobs that can’t be shipped overseas and ensure Wall Street pays a bit more of its fair share.
Today on Martin Luther King, Jr. Day, Dr. King’s legacy should remind us of how far we’ve come and how much work still lies ahead.
Dr. King also taught us that the moral arc of the universe bends toward justice. But that arc is long. It’s arduous. It’s uneven.
So we carry on in our efforts. And our fight isn’t over until every American is paid the living wage they’ve earned. Until everyone can afford health care and child care. Until everyone has power over their schedules and their careers and their lives. Until everyone can find a good job in their hometown.
Progress requires people like you and me to stand up, speak out, and get involved. Organizing, demanding progress, and working for change are some of the most patriotic things we can do. Because when you love this country, you fight for the people who make it work -- all people.
With gratitude,
Sherrod
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF SHERROD BROWN
Contributions or gifts to Friends of Sherrod Brown are not tax deductible.

3867 West Market Street
Ste. 289
Akron, OH 44333
United States

name: Katie Porter
subject: Priorities for fighting racial injustice and inequality
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=15805%2E3422442%2E_Apvx0)
Derek, today we honor and remember Martin Luther King Jr., who tirelessly fought for a more just and fair society.
But today and everyday, we must do more than make empty promises to fight for justice. Here are some of my priorities for addressing racial injustice and inequality:
**We should abolish the Senate filibuster,** and if elected to the Senate I’d be a strong advocate for doing so. The filibuster has time and time again been used to stop the Senate from passing meaningful civil rights legislation: pieces like the John Lewis Voting Rights Act and bills that would have helped get big money out of politics. The filibuster enables so-called leaders like Mitch McConnell to keep rigging the system against Black families.
**As a consumer protection advocate, I’ve seen how Black families are preyed upon by predatory lenders and how we can use policy to stop bad actors. In Congress I’ve worked to hold these bad actors accountable**. Our economy doesn’t work for too many Black families, and I’ll continue to fight special interests and big corporations that make it hard for people to get ahead.
There’s so much work to be done. Onward,
Katie Porter

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4s-emails/?t=2&akid=15805%2E3422442%2E_Apvx0)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s-em-update-info-footer)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4s-unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=15805%2E3422442%2E_Apvx0)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Senate
PO Box 5176
Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate

name: Aadland For Colorado
subject: An Important Announcement
body:
From Erik Aadland
| | | |
---
---
|
# Continuing The Fight
---
|
---
| | |
---|---
|
Dear friends,
I am greatly excited to announce that I have decided to run for Chairman of Colorado's Republican Party. With the door unexpectedly closing on my Congressional bid, another door opens, and given the dire state of Colorado and our Grand Old Party, I cannot quit the arena.

I am blessed to have run for Congress, to have received extraordinary support from across Colorado and our country. With that experience, I learned an extensive amount and established a phenomenal network of supporters; all this can be leveraged as State Republican Chairman to help turn our state around.

Perhaps many of you are tired and disheartened. Maybe you have even given up on the Republican Party here in Colorado. Given we all expected a red wave in 2022 and instead experienced a swath of defeats across Colorado; clearly, reality did not mirror our expectations. So, I understand if you feel defeated. Nevertheless, all is not lost, and the calling to fight for freedom, truth, our families and our God-given rights has never been so great.

Though it will not be easy, I seek to lead our party out of despair, to go on the offensive, and to take on the Radical Left destroying our state.

As our next Chairman, here are my top priorities:
## Win elections for Republicans.
---
This is the primary purpose of the Republican Party. In 2023, we will be hyper-focused on identifying unaffiliated voters on conservative issues and building our small donor base to be able to give our Republican candidates the financial and data support they need to win in 2024. A major part of this effort will be:
1. Developing and executing a long-term strategy to reclaim the state.
2. Regaining the Republican advantage in chasing ballots.
3. Identify the unaffiliated voters on conservative issues.
4. Rebuild the Republican small donor base to arm Republican candidates with the resources needed and to launch valuable voter contact programs focused on winning elections for Republicans.

---
---
|
## Protect our elections.
---
| Far too many people lack faith in our election system. Early in 2023, I will commission a committee to advise the chairman on how to best restore integrity to our election system in Colorado. I will ask this committee to report its recommendations to the chairman by September 1, 2023 in order to give the party an opportunity to place ballot initiatives on the 2024 ballot. The committee will be charged with considering all ideas for election reform including:
1. Holding our corrupt Secretary of State accountable,
2. Cleaning the voter rolls,
3. Making ballot harvesting illegal,
4. Implementing a trustworthy audit process,
5. Reverting to in-person voting,
6. Closing our primaries and,
7. Getting rid of the current voting machines.

---
## Unite the party with sound leadership
---
Listening to all camps, and finding the common ground necessary to convey a message attractive to Colorado voters, without compromising our conservative values. To this end, I will be a chairman for all Republicans (establishment and grassroots), recognizing that to take back lost ground, Republicans need to put on the same jersey and pull together. Our state and nation are at stake, and it will take every one of us.
---
I am a husband, father, battle-tested warrior, and former businessman with significant experience managing teams all over the world. I enter this race not for power, prestige, or wealth, but to be of service to our party, Colorado and this still great nation. Though we have lost some battles, we have not lost the war. However, Colorado is in peril, and we must join together like never before to turn the tide back to sound governance, which is for the people, not for special interests such as big tech, or in service to corrupt politicians like Joe Biden, Governor Polis, Jena Griswold, or Brittany Pettersen.
Together, we will prevail, and I am excited to continue to fight with you in the valiant cause. God bless you and God bless America!

---
|
---
|
---
| |
---
---

---
|
---
---
|
## About Erik
---
| |
---
| Jefferson County resident Erik Aadland is a West Point graduate and a twice-decorated Bronze Star veteran--one for valor--from his time in Iraq and Afghanistan. Concluding his army career in 2011 as a Captain, he pursued a career in the oil and natural gas industry with his work culminating as a project manager directing the onshore construction of the Leviathan Project in Dor, Israel. This project resulted in Israel being energy-independent and a net exporter of natural gas. Erik holds a master's degree in the field of psychology. He is a devoted husband and proud father of three children.

clean_body: From Erik Aadland | | | | | Continuing The Fight | | | | ---|--- | Dear friends, I am greatly excited to announce that I have decided to run for Chairman of Colorado's Republican Party. With the door unexpectedly closing on my Congressional bid, another door opens, and given the dire state of Colorado and our Grand Old Party, I cannot quit the arena. I am blessed to have run for Congress, to have received extraordinary support from across Colorado and our country. With that experience, I learned an extensive amount and established a phenomenal network of supporters; all this can be leveraged as State Republican Chairman to help turn our state around. Perhaps many of you are tired and disheartened. Maybe you have even given up on the Republican Party here in Colorado. Given we all expected a red wave in 2022 and instead experienced a swath of defeats across Colorado; clearly, reality did not mirror our expectations. So, I understand if you feel defeated. Nevertheless, all is not lost, and the calling to fight for freedom, truth, our families and our God-given rights has never been so great. Though it will not be easy, I seek to lead our party out of despair, to go on the offensive, and to take on the Radical Left destroying our state. As our next Chairman, here are my top priorities: Win elections for Republicans. This is the primary purpose of the Republican Party. In 2023, we will be hyper-focused on identifying unaffiliated voters on conservative issues and building our small donor base to be able to give our Republican candidates the financial and data support they need to win in 2024.
