clean_body: Are K-12 schools, colleges, and universities in your state doing enough to provide students with a proper understanding of American history and government | | | | | This sponsored message from Conservative Liberty Wire is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | | Fellow American, Are K-12 schools, colleges, and universities in your state doing enough to provide students with a proper understanding of American history and government--including an understanding of the purposes and principles of the Constitution? There is reason to be doubtful. There seems to be a decreasing sense of patriotism among young Americans, and an increasing attraction to socialism and other ideas destructive of liberty. And have you noticed all the disturbing incidents taking place on college campuses, with students resorting to intimidation, threats, and violence to silence speakers with whom they disagree? Hillsdale is planning to redouble its efforts for the future of liberty. We will respond to these disturbing trends by expanding our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty--especially for younger Americans. And as we form our strategy we'd like to hear from you: We have done too many of our young people a disservice by failing to provide them with knowledge of our nation's great heritage of liberty. Even worse, many of our schools, corrupted by Left-wing bias, have pushed a false history of America as essentially flawed and unjust. This indoctrination will only continue once students return to campuses across the country. Hillsdale can remain independent and stay true to its mission because it refuses to accept one penny of taxpayer funding--even indirect aid like federal or state student grants and loans. This frees Hillsdale from burdensome and corrupting regulation, but it also means we are entirely dependent on the support of our friends nationwide. Will you help us understand how Americans look at education today so that we can plan our expanded outreach in the smartest way possible? I am deeply grateful for your friendship. Warm Regards, Larry P. Arnn President, Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth - Defending Liberty since 1844 | | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA Sent to Conservative Liberty Wire subscribers. 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

name: George Ciampa, American Veterans Center
subject: The high price of freedom on D-Day
body:
On D-Day, I landed on Utah Beach as a skinny 18-year-old kid who'd never been away from home before.
| | | [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zn58d0962qvvsl8877bh797hq7bhn6kokvd234gg_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji60oj6c1h2bs)
---
Fellow American, I know the high price of freedom -- because I saw it paid in World War II. On D-Day, I landed on Utah Beach as a skinny 18-year-old kid who'd never been away from home before. As part of the 607th Graves Registration company, I was tasked with the solemn duty of burying the fallen. From D-Day ... to the Battle of the Bulge ... to the end of the war in Europe, I helped honor and inter some 75,000 soldiers. **I didn't talk about my time in World War II for nearly 50 years.** But today, I've produced and directed five World War II documentaries -- including the return of D-Day veterans like me to Normandy on the 75th anniversary -- and spoken to thousands of students of all ages. _I want the next generation to understand the high price of freedom, and I want to make sure they never forget the lessons of the World War._
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zbjot3mgstu8352cpjo4oeela6k70nr89lkl6a3o_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji60oj6c1h2bs)
---
_Above: My friends at the American Veterans Center released a 46-minute video documentary in May 2020 about my experience in World War II._
---
**I was proud to join my friends at the American Veterans Center (AVC) to film a 46-minute video documentary about my World War II experience.** No other organization does as much to record, preserve, and honor our veterans' stories and legacies than the American Veterans Center. _That's why, when my friends at AVC told me that they urgently needed to raise more funds for their work, I immediately said I would help._ [**Today I ask you, friend: will you make a generous tax-deductible contribution of $25, $50, $75, $100, $250, $500, or more to the American Veterans Center today? As a non-profit organization, AVC depends on the support of patriotic Americans like you.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z0s7hm52dmb71ns094gc1nrfhcklu824qmlhdt78_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji60oj6c1h2bs)
---
[Support the American Veterans Center](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z8f36fgnghtj1ak4h18066j0oqbb4p3rg29ivi6o_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji60oj6c1h2bs)
---
When I first returned home, my experience as a World War II veteran was not unusual. There were thousands of returning servicemen and servicewomen like me -- and, tragically, thousands more families who never got to welcome their loved ones home. **But today, Americans don't have the nationally shared experience of World War II, even though the lessons of patriotism, sacrifice, honor, duty, and courage are vital for every generation to learn.** [**That's why the work that the American Veterans Center does to preserve and pass on our veterans' legacies is so important. Can I count on you to chip in $25, $50, $75, $100, $250, $500, or more today to help support their work?**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zqju578ltuud8jot99u6933hnjjicl3an6ku4kig_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji60oj6c1h2bs) The American Veterans Center does more than any other group to fulfill the vital mission of safeguarding our veterans' legacies, including: • Honoring our fallen heroes with the nation's largest Memorial Day commemoration at the **National Memorial Day Parade** in Washington, D.C. • Bringing together veterans, from World War II to the War on Terror, with students and young military cadets at **AVC's Annual Conference** , an event I'm honored to have attended • Recording and producing high-quality **video documentaries with veterans** like me -- documentaries that become an essential part of American history, with the videos being used by the Library of Congress, presidential libraries, and more. _You now have a chance to play a critical role in preserving and passing on the stories of World War II veterans like me._ [**Please, friend -- make your most generous gift of $25, $50, $75, $100, $250, $500, or more today to help support the American Veterans Center.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z93tlc6rk3curnb4p5pnksqd97a3q3ov0v15609o_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji60oj6c1h2bs) Thank you,
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zjvqmjebh0bgqrd5tgl8mghvv9s2u5i26e5ehkc8_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji60oj6c1h2bs)
---
George Ciampa World War II Veteran For the American Veterans Center
---
[Support the American Veterans Center](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z6bm2nopl6qdc8rvsissoe1sdqh8rnhsl835kmfo_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji60oj6c1h2bs)
---
|
---
The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. © American Veterans Center
1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201

name: Carl Felton, American Veterans Center
subject: I'm one of America's last D-Day soldiers
body:
I left home at age 17 to fight in World War II.
| | | [](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zjk0qpas1hd4qlvud8q5tm6mnqm2t3rctrs5d040_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs)
---
Fellow American, I left home at age 17 to fight in World War II. After my initial training with the U.S. Navy – where I learned how to swim for the first time – I sailed from New York City on the _RMS Queen Mary_ , not knowing where I would be sent.
---
[](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2znv8avorh740e7joq52gou019rv11iraan7jqim0_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs)
---
_Above: My friends at the American Veterans Center recorded a video documentary about my experience in World War II. I was proud to share my story with the next generation._
---
**Months later, on D-Day, I was a half-mile off Omaha Beach on a ship, where I was working to coordinate communications for the invasion.** Later, I would learn the full scope of the D-Day plans, from the critical victories that Allied Forces achieved to the tremendous cost of brave soldiers’ lives. But on that day, with Allied planes flying overhead, Nazi artillery shells coming off the beach, and keeping up with rapid Morse code messages, I focused on my part in the broader mission. **Nearly 75 years later, I was proud to share my experience at D-Day and my time serving our country in a video documentary with my friends at the American Veterans Center.** As I’ve learned first-hand, no other organization is doing as much as the American Veterans Center for the legacies of World War II veterans like me. _That's why, when my friends at the American Veterans Center explained that they needed help to continue raising financial support for their critically important work, I was eager to write to you today._ [**Friend, will you make a generous tax-deductible contribution of $25, $50, $75, $100, $250, $500, or more to the American Veterans Center right now? Your support will mean a great deal to AVC** ’**s mission -- and to me personally, too.**](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zcqmo3imc8l1khg9i7visilrt8g345konhio7ovg_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs)
---
[Support the American Veterans Center](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zrj1rv2k96u66d1ee0oa7gpgi5pa6h1pvshvqrp8_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs)
---
I’ve experienced many of the American Veterans Center’s outstanding programs first-hand, including:
* **AVC** ’**s Annual Conference** , which brings together American veterans from all generations to speak with hundreds of students and military cadets. I’m proud to see the legacy of the World War II generation passed on directly to our young people.

* **The National Memorial Day Parade** in Washington, D.C., which the American Veterans Center brought back as a proud tradition after it had lain dormant for decades. The National Memorial Day Parade is our nation’s largest Memorial Day commemoration, with participants from my World War II generation to active-duty servicemembers today.

* **AVC** ’**s video documentary project,** which records veterans’ stories of their service – in their own words – to enter our shared historical record. I was honored to film a video documentary with the American Veterans Center, and it means a great deal to know that it has been watched by thousands online and will be made available to groups like the Library of Congress, presidential libraries, and World War II and military historians.**
**

**As a non-profit organization, the American Veterans Center depends on the support of patriotic Americans like you.** [**Can my fellow World War II veterans and I count on you to help AVC preserve our legacy with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $100, $250, $500, or more today?**](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z2ahnkvaea300u07culdtc28j68nhcpmjv6n5bg0_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs)
---
[Support the American Veterans Center](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z5dogt243kq0knjr0m39mp9hjgfg2d0m1k6a1ifo_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs)
---
I’ve always been keenly aware of the great sacrifice made by American soldiers, sailors, and airmen on D-Day – and the families who never got to welcome their loved ones home. **As one of the last D-Day soldiers in America today, it** ’**s my honor to partner with the American Veterans Center to honor the memory of the men and women who served with me in World War II.** But for AVC to continue their critical programs, they urgently need the support of patriotic, service-minded Americans like you. [**Please, if I can count on you – make your most generous gift of $25, $50, $75, $100, $250, $500, or more to help support the American Veteran Center** ’**s work to honor veterans** ’ **legacies today.**](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zvu1l1ilmurnhurkb560i4stsaiekvca2a0s4260_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs) Thank you,
---
[](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z54kidof4nrnlfccioe4r92js4o5rq21nlquf848_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs)
---
Carl Felton World War II Veteran For the American Veterans Center
---
[Support the American Veterans Center](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2znfftib164a9pjmq3i4k7vbqem2gta9a5852hu3g_rp22sh2s8i66pr6cji68o35c1h2bs)
---
|
---
The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. © American Veterans Center
1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201
---

I was honored to film a video documentary with the American Veterans Center, and it means a great deal to know that it has been watched by thousands online and will be made available to groups like the Library of Congress, presidential libraries, and World War II and military historians. As a non-profit organization, the American Veterans Center depends on the support of patriotic Americans like you. I’ve always been keenly aware of the great sacrifice made by American soldiers, sailors, and airmen on D-Day – and the families who never got to welcome their loved ones home. As one of the last D-Day soldiers in America today, it ’s my honor to partner with the American Veterans Center to honor the memory of the men and women who served with me in World War II.** But for AVC to continue their critical programs, they urgently need the support of patriotic, service-minded Americans like you. Thank you, Carl Felton World War II Veteran For the American Veterans Center | The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. © American Veterans Center 1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201 If dpwillis67@gmail.com should not be subscribed or if you need to change your subscription information for Deroy Murdock, . 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

body:

---
Fellow Patriot, I want to send you a FREE copy of my newest book, _How We Win!_ **While supplies last, you can get a copy of this brand-new book to give to a conservative friend or family member (or to add to your personal library) when you make a gift of _ANY AMOUNT_ to Turning Point USA.**
---
[](http://trk.freedomfirstproject.)
---
[**Just click here to make your gift and claim your copy before it's gone!**](http://trk.freedomfirstproject.)
---
[ Get my book](http://trk.freedomfirstproject.-L-PGYaT33I_lcA)
---
Until now, Turning Point USA has been giving this book away as a thank-you gift for donations of $100 or more... **...but generous TPUSA supporters found the message of _How We Win_ to be so _URGENT_ and so _IMPORTANT_ that they're helping me get it into the hands of as many grassroots patriots as possible.** [**I hope you'll make your gift to Turning Point USA right now and claim your copy of _How We Win_ before it's gone. Let me tell you -- these books are flying out of our warehouse!**](http://trk.freedomfirstproject.) _How We Win_ is my prescription for a new conservative movement that will SAVE America once and for all. You see, after TEN YEARS at the helm of Turning Point USA... ... TEN YEARS criss-crossing the country... ... TEN YEARS engaging with the good, the bad, and the ugly on college campuses... ... I have come to the conclusion that the time has passed where conservatives can hope to simply persuade liberal radicals to come around to our way of thinking. That simply isn't going to happen. We must become a movement that's unafraid to stand up for what's right... a movement that's done pulling punches and will _FIGHT_ for the moral and philosophical foundations that have made our nation the greatest in the history of the world. **_How We Win_ is my handbook for how we can become that movement -- and how we can secure victories for American Greatness that will last for generations to come.** And this special book can be YOURS for a gift of ANY AMOUNT today! [**Please -- if you haven't claimed your copy already, click here to get it now.**](http://trk.freedomfirstproject.)
---
[ Get my book](http://trk.freedomfirstproject.-C97J6wjg)
---
Even a gift of just $5 will help us ship the book to you and get this vitally important knowledge into the hands of patriots all across America. And best of all, when you make a generous gift today, you can rest assured that you're doing more than just getting a book. You're investing in the most powerful conservative youth organization in America. You see, with over 500,000 patriotic high school and college students on over 3,000 campuses nationwide, TPUSA isn't just _talking_ about saving America... **... we're _DOING IT!_** With the partnership of friends like you, we're helping high school and college students love America again. We're helping to restore patriotism and love of country as a way of life for hundreds of thousands of young Americans every year and changing the trajectory of a generation. This is your invitation to participate in that nation-saving work, my friend. [**It all starts when you claim your copy of How We Win -- which can be YOURS for a gift of any amount today!**](http://trk.freedomfirstproject.-SiVPbuHHQ)
---
| | [ Get my book ](http://trk.freedomfirstproject.)
---
For America,
---
Charlie Kirk Founder & CEOTurning Point USA

Phoenix, Arizona 85044

name: =?utf-8?Q?Lavern=20Spicer?=
subject: My brother, Martin, was a conservative.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Right Hook PAC
body:
| | | [](https://unitedconservativesamerica.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=22432bec7f541233c6007cfc2&id=263b0d20a9&e=fdefed58de)
---
Hello Peter,
Today, I write to you on the day that we honor Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. For decades, Democrats have co-opted his life and legacy but most of his teachings would appeal to modern Conservatives.Dr. King believed that all Americans should be guided by the self-determination secured by our Founding Fathers and the Declaration of Independence: the rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness. Sounds like a soundbite from Donald Trump, doesn’t it? You are right! **These views from MLK are very Republican sounding.** But leftists want to silence any messaging from a Black American that might speak to Conservative values.That’s why the Left is trying to silence me but like Dr. King, I will not go quietly. I am going to keep fighting for my community and keep fighting for the Republican Party to win communities like mine.
| [Support Lavern’s Fight!](https://unitedconservativesamerica.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=22432bec7f541233c6007cfc2&id=8d141f60f4&e=fdefed58de)
---
Last year, I started Right Hook PAC because I saw that we needed more Black voices in Republican politics. Most Black people don’t realize it, but they are more Conservative than they think they are. Unfortunately, the Democrat machine has been taking advantage of Black voters for generations.That’s where Right Hook PAC comes in. I want to use this PAC to support Black candidates and to educate the Black community about Conservative politics.[**WILL YOU RUSH IN SUPPORT TODAY TO HONOR DR. KING AND HELP ME SPREAD THE WORD IN THE BLACK COMMUNITY FOR 2024?**](https://unitedconservativesamerica.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=22432bec7f541233c6007cfc2&id=12f12db12b&e=fdefed58de) So while modern Conservatives might not espouse every one of Dr. King’s beliefs, many were hallmarks of the Right. There was always a deeply conservative message throughout Dr. King's speeches and teachings.That’s the messaging I want future Black candidates and voters to hear. clean_body: | | | Hello Peter, Today, I write to you on the day that we honor Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. For decades, Democrats have co-opted his life and legacy but most of his teachings would appeal to modern Conservatives.Dr. King believed that all Americans should be guided by the self-determination secured by our Founding Fathers and the Declaration of Independence: the rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness. Sounds like a soundbite from Donald Trump, doesn’t it? You are right! These views from MLK are very Republican sounding. But leftists want to silence any messaging from a Black American that might speak to Conservative values.That’s why the Left is trying to silence me but like Dr. King, I will not go quietly. I am going to keep fighting for my community and keep fighting for the Republican Party to win communities like mine. | Last year, I started Right Hook PAC because I saw that we needed more Black voices in Republican politics. Most Black people don’t realize it, but they are more Conservative than they think they are. Unfortunately, the Democrat machine has been taking advantage of Black voters for generations.That’s where Right Hook PAC comes in. I want to use this PAC to support Black candidates and to educate the Black community about Conservative politics. So while modern Conservatives might not espouse every one of Dr. King’s beliefs, many were hallmarks of the Right. There was always a deeply conservative message throughout Dr. King's speeches and teachings.That’s the messaging I want future Black candidates and voters to hear. Help me get the word out in the community by contributing today. | With Love,Lavern Spicer | Paid for by Right Hook PAC | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2023 American Conservatives United. name: American Veterans Center
subject: With not much time left, can you help us tell this WWII hero's story?
body:
I have an urgent request -- and since I know you care about our nation's heroes, I wanted to reach out to you right away. [](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/BC.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c210334%7c0464%7c8%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c805&wdmdk=KW%2bzvrHceYIVqOfAeH9yYw&sysid=1)
| | | [](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c656962%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=kmywp3nvzmaR2BVaBCJWHA&sysid=1)
---
Fellow American, I have an urgent request -- and since I know you care about our nation's heroes, I wanted to reach out to you right away. Jack Nakamura, a World War II veteran of the legendary 442nd Regimental Combat Team, wants to share his story with the American Veterans Center so we can preserve his legacy for the next generation. **_To film, produce, and release a documentary with this World War II hero, the American Veterans Center must urgently raise $1,583._** Will you make a gift to this emergency fundraising campaign right now? >> **[_https://secure.anedot.com/american-veterans-center_](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c656963%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=biaA1%2bBMp2sZ%2blTo%2b8ypEw&sysid=1)** Jack, who will celebrate his 100th birthday in February, is one of the last surviving members of the all-Japanese-American 442nd Regimental Combat Team. In October 1944, the 442nd saved more than 200 American soldiers of the "Lost Battalion" that had been cut off from Allied communications and surrounded by Nazis in the mountains of eastern France. **Today, Jack is one of the few remaining American heroes who can tell this story from first-hand experience --_and I don't want to miss the opportunity to preserve his lessons and experience._** Fellow American, because you're a strong supporter of America's veterans, I'm hoping that I can count on your help for this emergency request before the holiday season. I hope to be in touch soon with updates about our interview with Jack -- because _YOUR_ support is what makes it possible. \-- James Roberts, American Veterans Center
---
[**Support the American Veterans Center**](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c656964%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=LVbDTK6pyICNP7jsoHq%2bbg&sysid=1)
---
The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. (C) American Veterans Center

body:

|

More than half of the GOP members who repeatedly blocked the selection of a House Speaker are from states with gerrymandered maps. Because map manipulation all but guarantees their reelection, politicians like Matt Gaetz, Marjorie Taylor Greene, and Jim Jordan can focus on energizing the fringe elements of their base rather than on governing.

**The NDRC is fighting to end gerrymandering so Democrats can win back the House, put an end to this chaos, and get back to work on delivering progress for the American people.[_Will you pitch in now to support our efforts to secure fair elections and a functioning democracy?_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/57405052/387824002/-276754120?amount=30&refcode=20230116.1r_527&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhhYWQ0ZTY2LWQ4OTUtZWQxMS05ZDdiLTAwMjI0ODMyZTgxMSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=ZKFZCtdXXNmaHBBjCapG6es90rmOtDIdZauGFcE3_gg=)**

clean_body: Team, for the first time in 100 years, the House of Representatives failed to quickly select a speaker – and we have gerrymandering to thank for that. | Team, for the first time in 100 years, the House of Representatives failed to quickly select a speaker – and we have gerrymandering to thank for that. | = = More than half of the GOP members who repeatedly blocked the selection of a House Speaker are from states with gerrymandered maps. Because map manipulation all but guarantees their reelection, politicians like Matt Gaetz, Marjorie Taylor Greene, and Jim Jordan can focus on energizing the fringe elements of their base rather than on governing. The NDRC is fighting to end gerrymandering so Democrats can win back the House, put an end to this chaos, and get back to work on delivering progress for the American people.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = – The NDRC Team = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. name: Jennifer Wexton
subject: Realizing Dr. King's dream
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wexton for Congress
body:
It didn't take long for the House GOP to make noise about causing a government shutdown

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57405257/387825568/-876189841?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlY2MyZTgxNS1kOTk1LWVkMTEtOWQ3Yi0wMDIyNDgzMmU4MTEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kcm5DVTh6j8Jc-F96t6-lUZL3ba4f488nW4An6XEHBc=&emci=c3f181dd-cd95-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&emdi=ecc2e815-d995-ed11-9d7b-00224832e811&ceid=32654120)
---
Friend,

I have always found these words by Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. to be especially powerful: _“I believe that unarmed truth and unconditional love will have the final word in reality. This is why right, temporarily defeated, is stronger than evil triumphant.”_ Throughout his unparalleled fight for racial equality, civil rights, and voting rights in the United States, Dr. King spoke eloquently about the arc of the moral universe. He recognized that progress would not be measured in simple linear terms, and he was undeterred. He believed that justice would prevail in the end.

For every great stride we make toward realizing the dream that Dr. King dedicated his life toward, we often discover new barriers — and we even sometimes see progress rolled back.

We must remain dedicated and fearless in the fight for equal justice. We cannot grow overconfident from our victories, and we cannot get lost in despair from our losses.

Today, on Martin Luther King, Jr. Day, we recommit to making his words a reality. We acknowledge the fierce urgency of now and the importance of acts of love and compassion in the face of hate. And we pledge to honor Dr. King's life by doing all we can to ensure that the moral arc of the universe will indeed, in the end, bend towards justice.

Sincerely,

Jennifer
Paid for by Wexton for Congress
Wexton for Congress
PO Box 650550
Sterling, VA 20165
United States

# [Reclaiming the radical legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=96911186&utm_source=post-email-title&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTY5MTExODYsImlhdCI6MTY3MzkwMDU5MSwiZXhwIjoxNjc2NDkyNTkxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.7w36MS4P4vrzatzawnxucrm1U0lkgXkavTnQSAho06U)
### We continue today to struggle for the objectives sought by Dr. King's movement, including universal healthcare, the right to food, the end of state violence, and freedom from militarism.
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/redirect/189436de-8c73-4f8e-ad0a-9d68011b58ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

---
Every year, our nation celebrates a holiday dedicated to the memory of a modern day prophet, international hero, voice for peace, and (as he is often described by many unaware of his broader legacy) civil rights leader. Washington erected a monument depicting his likeness situated among others commemorating presidents and world wars. Yet, at the same time that so many voices rightfully praise Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., most also manage to ignore the continuing significance of his voice, his experience, and his legacy.
Dr. King was far more than the feel-good icon of a victorious struggle for civil rights to which he has been widely reduced. Like previous figures from Jesus to Galileo, he saw through the illusions of this world and his era, explained truths that others were either too ignorant or complicit to recognize, and paid the ultimate price for doing so.
If more Americans came to recognize the continuing relevance of Dr. King's voice--not to history, but to current events--we might finally make some progress on the underlying goals of his movement that were never satisfied.
Even though Washington formally commemorates Dr. King's memory every year, few today recognize the [profound depth](https://substack.com/redirect/b6153d93-47ac-4ad6-8ab5-c744e863cc55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of his voice, or its [continuing relevance](https://substack.com/redirect/49945509-89c2-4d8e-9d44-151cd7e06051?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to U.S. politics. In no uncertain terms, he identified over 50 years ago [precisely](https://substack.com/redirect/e2559852-f5cd-4b24-8a46-da4718a99eec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the same [patterns](https://substack.com/redirect/3e38802d-4cea-4327-9ab1-f3651ea910ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that continue to besiege democracy in America today. Even the struggle to secure the national holiday in his memory offers lessons, particularly about the power of music to drive cultural dialog, and also the limits of that power when channeled towards policy goals.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=96911186&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTY5MTExODYsImlhdCI6MTY3MzkwMDU5MSwiZXhwIjoxNjc2NDkyNTkxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.7w36MS4P4vrzatzawnxucrm1U0lkgXkavTnQSAho06U)
### **MLK 's experience**
I wrote an article in 2017 titled "Remember Dr. King--and [What He Endured](https://substack.com/redirect/233cfc43-291f-414f-aacd-529d1cb03d86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" published by the Electronic Frontier Foundation, where I worked at the time. It explained how MLK's unfortunate experiences offer lessons with continuing relevance, even setting aside (for now) his voice and broader legacy.
> While we remember Dr. King's many achievements today, we also must remember the [documented and unfounded vilification](https://substack.com/redirect/b5c291d1-c7e6-4099-bca7-4c48269b2b6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by U.S. intelligence agencies that he, and others in the civil rights movement, endured....
> **A movement in Memoriam**
> The emergence of the [civil rights movement](https://substack.com/redirect/2fb611c1-74b6-4d2e-b0f8-b5287c633038?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the 1960s, its triumph over hate to establish desegregation and secure procedural voting rights, and the narrative of interracial struggle for justice--all reflect an inspiring legacy of a grassroots movement that aspired to hold America true to our founding values. As [Dr. King succinctly exhorted](https://substack.com/redirect/7549caa2-e019-44a6-ab67-28b87ec8d617?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the movement called on America to "Be true to what you said on paper."
> The movement was subjected to brutal violence, both by the [assassination of its leaders](https://substack.com/redirect/93d0cbb0-274c-421a-8724-77bcc7493ee4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and by the daily [brutality of police and vigilantes](https://substack.com/redirect/3dbc0f37-84e3-4676-a1fc-00b26eb42ef0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reacting to the desegregation of public institutions. Dozens of civil rights activists from various backgrounds were [murdered](https://substack.com/redirect/e2edd351-4d3c-4cac-a9ce-e89cfdbd1b12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) during this era, alongside hundreds--if not thousands--of African-Americans as young as 14 year-old [Emmitt Till](https://substack.com/redirect/0ef0215d-d352-4dc4-9594-a830208c5bbe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and 11 year-old [Denise McNair](https://substack.com/redirect/bc2a7f88-bf1c-4a79-82c4-f707aa47ac86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), whose church in Alabama was bombed by extremists using violent terror to oppose racial integration....
> **Violent state suppression of speech**
> Throughout Dr. King's life, and for a decade ([if not longer](https://substack.com/redirect/32d234d5-075a-4ed9-bc01-b46136660cfb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) beyond it, the FBI pursued what members of the U.S. Senate in 1976 described as "[a sophisticated vigilante operation](https://substack.com/redirect/4bf1d9c6-e2ba-4e91-a1f3-e1e441525834?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) aimed squarely at suppressing...First Amendment rights of speech and association." Those operations, described in internal FBI files as COINTELPRO, have been forgotten by many Americans, but represent a key to understanding why the specter of mass surveillance threatens not only privacy, but [also democracy](https://substack.com/redirect/e68128be-90a1-4a37-9315-bf3391485344?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> For 40 years, FBI Director J. Edgar Hoover presided over a [reign of intimidation and terror](https://substack.com/redirect/2d6d6d7a-ae1f-4a88-9196-511bd12659a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across Washington. Under his tenure, the FBI [blackmailed members of Congress](https://substack.com/redirect/06e68706-bd84-4db2-b0e9-ad0345d0a676?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and infiltrated organizations seeking everything from international peace to [equal rights for women](https://substack.com/redirect/0dde9d44-6b23-4c44-8ade-1cdf3476e73a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> The Bureau's aim was not to guard national security from any external threat, but instead to "[neutralize](https://substack.com/redirect/b9543dfa-c93c-437a-b592-0e7c50b3d053?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" constitutionally protected domestic dissent and people using their rights--including Dr. King. In addition to bugging his hotel rooms, monitoring his movements, and recording his liaisons, the FBI also tried to break up Dr. King's marriage and [attempted to prompt his suicide](https://substack.com/redirect/04c17ce4-a8eb-4618-a871-ed1f964ef8d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> Many Americans reacted to [seemingly politicized FBI disclosures](https://substack.com/redirect/8af5e426-fdf8-430b-9c35-351a01effa56?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the days before the 2016 presidential election with surprise. But the FBI has embroiled itself in partisan controversies since its very origins. From the [Palmer Raids](https://substack.com/redirect/7e6fb250-e478-42d8-b62a-9d9cbc797450?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) through the [McCarthy era](https://substack.com/redirect/1b2a853b-233f-47c9-984d-8d94ef39ee5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), from the [Green Scare](https://substack.com/redirect/bc1177b4-64d5-470a-80b8-9a412bdfa9ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to its infiltration of [labor organizing by farm workers](https://substack.com/redirect/3f0af7f2-cf35-4ad0-a608-d64f8f919a6a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the FBI has a long history of investigating and undermining constitutional rights in the context of political movements.
> Under Hoover's direction, the FBI achieved its written goal: the "neutralization" of domestic social groups speaking out to advance their views as protected by the First Amendment. Hoover's FBI achieved its goals with a fraction of the budget, staff--and none of the computing power--of the FBI today.
I wrote another article addressing Dr. King's legacy in 2017, this time for Truthout, titled [Elusive Victories: Voting Rights, Desegregation and the Erosion of Civil Rights](https://substack.com/redirect/37caa641-ef75-4c0b-b4ac-40634f108555?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It explains how, contrary to popular belief, the movement that Dr. King helped lead and represent half a century ago was less than successful in achieving its goal than most people today think.
> Ultimately, the civil rights movement was denied its most visionary goals by a policy establishment unwilling to grant them.
> Civil rights leaders had hoped to secure a [right to employment](https://substack.com/redirect/57f458fd-6d5b-4e62-abd6-a57eefafbf74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [food](https://substack.com/redirect/6c978835-d636-462b-9324-13eb65a6916c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [housing](https://substack.com/redirect/bd3c0e9d-1d34-4e5f-be75-66d755589ef2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [education](https://substack.com/redirect/db511161-4891-4091-a409-c6186085a494?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [health care](https://substack.com/redirect/588d9cf2-b3e3-4d19-bd40-18ebb134f444?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- all of which were goals that Dr. King explicitly referenced. Among its various goals, desegregation and voting rights were the only two ultimately secured in law and policy.
> The two victories that the civil rights movement did secure have withered in the decades since then. Voting rights, for example, have [dramatically eroded](https://substack.com/redirect/cb8c1be4-8edb-4b62-ace8-7816630f40a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from their high-water mark....
> Narratives describing the historic victories for voting rights obscure the ultimate reality. What few gains the civil rights movement did secure, unfortunately, on the most part did not survive.
[Elusive Victories](https://substack.com/redirect/37caa641-ef75-4c0b-b4ac-40634f108555?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) goes on to examine the history and fate of desegregation, which was judicially imposed in the 1950s before the courts later flipped the 14th Amendment on its head to more recently prevent efforts seeking equity in public opportunities.
> In _Brown v. Board of Education of Topeka_ (1954), the Supreme Court rejected de facto housing discrimination as a justification for unequal educational opportunities. More than 60 years ago, the Court recognized that "segregation is a denial of the equal protection of the laws," and required interdistrict busing between school districts to ensure racial integration.
> Fifty-seven years later, the Court effectively reversed itself.
> In 2007, two new justices (Chief Justice Roberts and Justice Alito) joined the Supreme Court, having been appointed by a president [whose own legitimacy was suspect](https://substack.com/redirect/9eb63ee7-f55e-4488-9a8d-9d00d48ec372?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). One of the first decisions in which they participated effectively overturned _Brown_ , without claiming to do so.
> The 2007 _Parents Involved_ decision prohibited public school districts from pursuing voluntary intradistrict busing plans to prevent racial isolation -- prohibiting essentially what _Brown_ had historically mandated. While perversely citing _Brown_ , the Roberts Court eviscerated its holding and left _Brown_ a doctrinally dangling shadow of its former self. As Justice Stevens wrote in his dissent from the majority decision, "[The chief justice's reliance on our decision in ](https://substack.com/redirect/1c33e976-d1c6-4467-a956-e2d1cd597cb2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[Brown](https://substack.com/redirect/1c33e976-d1c6-4467-a956-e2d1cd597cb2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ ," entails "a cruel irony."
> Today, de facto segregation in the housing market routinely determines which public schools students have an opportunity to attend. It also drives the experiences of young people with respect to police, who are deployed across the US, particularly in low-income communities of color, and who behave very differently in those settings than when policing high-income areas or examining white suspects.
The few goals that the civil rights movement did secure for a time have been either abandoned by policymakers & jurists (like voting rights and desegregation) or instead turned on their heads (like anti-discrimination principles, which conservatives [weaponized](https://substack.com/redirect/f58a11bc-5c4c-4976-bda1-29cbb3f3e756?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to challenge and overturn programs allowing preferential access for minorities to opportunities such as public education or employment).
We continue today to struggle for the broader objectives sought by his movement, including universal healthcare, the right to food, the end of state violence, and freedom from militarism.
### MLK's Voice
Dr. King's voice is remembered in many ways. Some of them--like reflexive invocations of his 1963 speech at the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom, in which he declared, "I have a dream"--ironically water down the broader range that his voice attained across contexts.
#### A letter from jail
Two texts, in particular, help reveal the radical perspective of this still underappreciated thinker. One was written while he was in jail in Alabama, just four months before his historic speech in Washington for which he is most widely remembered.
While many proud moderates might think of themselves as making reasoned compromises between opposing extremes, King explained how they in fact enable the brutality of the right wing by siding with it--in the name of order--against grassroots movements in every generation clamoring for long overdue justice.
Dr. King's iconic [Letter from Birmingham Jail](https://substack.com/redirect/e2559852-f5cd-4b24-8a46-da4718a99eec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) offers a succinct and compelling critique of deference to an establishment for the sake of expediency. He wrote to white clergy who had argued against King's organizing and agitation for racial justice. He explained the intrinsic urgency behind any struggle for justice, and how the preference of more comfortable allies for "order" leads many to resign, abandon, or simply forget their commitments to justice.
King explains how the intransigence of unapologetic racists should be expected. There is little, after all, that can be done to shift their attitudes.
But he saves a sharper critique for the so-called "ally" who disappears at the first sign of agitation. King describes "the white moderate" as playing the role of impeding justice, despite their intentions.
While many proud moderates might think of themselves as making reasoned compromises between opposing extremes, King explained how they in fact enable the brutality of the right wing by siding with it--in the name of order--against grassroots movements in every generation clamoring for long overdue justice.
The dynamic that Dr. King described in his letter from Birmingham Jail is unfortunately timeless.
Many voices, for instance, have responded to the contemporary movement for Black lives, and calls to abolish policing and mass slavery through incarceration, by emphasizing how poorly the slogan "defund the police" [polls](https://substack.com/redirect/a4b16d3b-839f-4658-91ae-caa7f31674ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with public audiences. They either never learned, or chose to ignore, Dr. King's teaching.
To reduce demands for justice to slogans, and evaluate them based on public opinion--as opposed to the moral and legal principles at stake, as courts as supposed to do--is to abandon the rule of law altogether.
When the Supreme Court took action to end Jim Crow, it didn't do it by deferring to the established order. Desegregation was deeply unpopular in 1954, so much so that after the Court issued its ruling in _Brown_ , state officials openly tried to nullify the case by invoking state rights, prompting a series of further [decisions](https://substack.com/redirect/4c6249fe-3895-4c61-8e7f-e9d5ff338096?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (including one of the [only](https://substack.com/redirect/9ce85ca2-6958-4a2c-b2bc-7a3df36fc1bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) unanimous joint opinions in the Supreme Court's 250-year history) that ultimately forced the schools in Little Rock, Arkansas, to finally desegregate four years later. It took yet [another](https://substack.com/redirect/3cb8687d-5f1e-43b9-98b6-d6415b3c924c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) case to force the schools open after local officials tried to close them altogether.
Today, nearly a century later, our country continues to wage a [cultural war](https://substack.com/redirect/f8ebae29-1fc6-4d62-acfb-5ca676011ba9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) over the vision animating those rulings, while [pretending](https://substack.com/redirect/76d8d476-05a1-4995-a928-5cdecb6834da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to have made progress on issues that in fact reflect stagnation and [retrogression](https://substack.com/redirect/c7c4d171-37ea-48a4-96e6-9f31354fe1cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The deference shown by "the white moderate" towards power, motivated by a need to preserve the establishment's order in spite of its thorough illegitimacy, remains the central challenge confronting movements for justice in the United States, as well as those around the world responding to abuses enabled by Washington.
Four months after writing his letter from Birmingham Jail, King spoke in Washington of [his dream envisioning a post-racial society](https://substack.com/redirect/c69362e4-71fe-4359-918d-20e3c7dac22c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). That vision has armed legions of subsequent white moderates with the quotes necessary to defang King's critique--especially in the wake of the [symbolic triumphs](https://substack.com/redirect/8467dfcb-9e50-4756-8fcb-3da2a08832d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to which President Obama reduced his own legacy. That is why the Letter from Birmingham Jail ranks among the most crucial political texts in U.S. history. It exposes the true voice of an international icon widely reduced to caricature.
As we shall see, that truth remains crucial to expose today. In fact, it has grown only more urgent as the injustice to which King responded has expanded dramatically, both here in the U.S. and across the entire world.
#### A speech identifying three "intersecting evils"
Four years (almost to the day) after his historic speech in Washington, King [spoke in Chicago at the National Conference for New Politics](https://substack.com/redirect/141bf4ca-c950-4404-a4cc-36acd1faaebe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). This address was incredibly significant, both because he coined an analytical method that gained a widespread following only decades later, and also because he specifically named three plagues that continue to besiege democracy in America--and life on Earth--today.
King described how seemingly separate social phenomena reinforce each other. Racism refers to systems of entrenched hierarchy, while capitalism is an economic model that bases its legitimacy on claims to meritocracy that should seemingly align with an anti-racist perspective.
Meanwhile, militarism emerges through spending priorities, foreign policy, and a culture dedicated to glorifying war & conflict in forums from movies to video games.
The problems of racism and militarism may seem separate from a country's economic model, but King spoke--in no uncertain terms--about how militarism, capitalism, and racism inevitably "intersect."
It's not hard to see why.
Capitalism is predicated on the ability of investors to invest capital in order to seek investment income or capital appreciation. It is necessarily path dependent, in the sense that anyone with greater resources at any given moment in time enjoys massive advantages over others who do not, particularly in the ability to leverage existing capital, and the ability to decline to transact at all.
Yet, despite claims that it somehow advances meritocracy, the contemporary distribution of wealth--capital--reflects generations of theft, appropriation, genocide, forced labor, and war. It has been dramatically skewed by a long history of violent racism by colonial powers: the militarism that King described as another intersecting evil.
Colonialism is not merely a historical phenomenon, but in fact has continuing aspects that all tend to reinforce white capital. The most direct example may be [discriminatory assessments](https://substack.com/redirect/4ee15d26-9ce9-438d-9d6d-baa2de8bac8e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in housing markets, but the dynamic repeats itself in [hiring](https://substack.com/redirect/be3d0fb3-4f14-483b-bb0a-ee34ecf1c9ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), advancement, and any number of other spheres from [education](https://substack.com/redirect/55f7c5fc-9393-4517-967e-ba507941d9cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to [agriculture](https://substack.com/redirect/caa5056c-0d20-420b-b88c-76d1d38d0902?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [technology](https://substack.com/redirect/424fea18-859c-4efa-8fee-0fea94dc37e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Dr. King himself spoke about how even constructions of beauty and language have been warped by dimensions of racism that continue to loom over our country today.
[The Martin Luther King, Jr. Center @TheKingCenter"I'm Black and Beautiful!" #MLK ](https://substack.com/redirect/a2f18984-7388-4240-84ce-67500fe38292?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[7:59 PM ∙ Jan 15, 2023
* * *
1,160Likes355Retweets](https://substack.com/redirect/a2f18984-7388-4240-84ce-67500fe38292?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In addition to substantively identifying racism, capitalism, and militarism as the evils poisoning America and its role in the world, King also pioneered an analytical method that at the time was revolutionary.
Rather than analyze each of those social conditions in isolation, he observed how they each serve as reinforcing causes and effects for the others. This holistic approach to considering the implications of policies, and the paradigms they together construct, stands in sharp contrast to the dominant analytical method that instead aims to isolate issues and divorce discussions from context or history.
Journalists learned nothing from King. In 2011, when an intersectional movement responding to his vision reclaimed public space in cities across America to challenge economic inequality, forever wars, and ecocide on behalf of the 99%, the same canards that King faced were deployed once again against us.
In particular, the press demonstrated its ignorance of intersectionality by foolishly insisting that the movement reduce its [various intersectional demands](https://substack.com/redirect/92a8af87-856e-44db-8eaa-d8af7a9c82f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to bullet points. Editors dutifully played the role of "the white moderate" decried by MLK himself.
Over the years since the Occupy movement once again inspired a generation of American justice activists, the notion of intersectionality has gained increasingly [widespread recognition](https://substack.com/redirect/a586a1e0-3d2d-4de5-83cc-353d062b3a4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). I was proud to help pioneer its application to grassroots organizing against policing abuses starting in 2010, by developing [intersectional goals](https://substack.com/redirect/52ac305f-b1d7-4b8d-8804-f0f493d41236?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that combined the objectives of various grassroots movements, crafting [information resources](https://substack.com/redirect/5f96dabc-f113-4176-b90f-261703679fb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to support grassroots coalitions, and [cultivating them](https://substack.com/redirect/ca4bcbc8-3b3c-4a98-8643-5a9e1cbf835e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across the U.S. for several years.
I later grew dismayed to discover how many [voices](https://substack.com/redirect/c45d5c49-83a8-4687-83d5-529b50b13394?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) loyal to the Democratic establishment get away with mouthing concerns about intersectionality while ultimately punching down in the [service](https://substack.com/redirect/459acc91-e543-4935-92d6-2776689b26e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of corruption.
### MLK's Legacy
Describing King as merely a "civil rights leader" is offensive for at least two reasons. First, his voice addressed a vast array of issues well beyond civil rights, including economics, foreign policy, and jurisprudence. Second, he was a leader not only of a movement, but an entire civilization.
If more Americans came to recognize the continuing relevance of Dr. King's voice--not to history, but to current events--we might finally make some progress on the underlying goals of his movement that were never satisfied.
Not only do we have yet to secure the goals that King explicitly sought, but we today carry a further burden: overturning the impacts of a mounting global crisis whose precursors he observed, only to be ignored.
Beyond offering vital insights on how our seemingly separate struggles ultimately intersect, King also [presaged](https://substack.com/redirect/b6153d93-47ac-4ad6-8ab5-c744e863cc55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the mounting global climate catastrophe.
First, his own stated concerns centered on racial justice, economic justice, and global peace & justice--each of which informs the contemporary movement for climate justice. To the same extent that intersectional analysis informed his identification of the "evils" that we have yet to overcome, it has also revealed in the years since his assassination just how dangerous the threats he discussed then have come to grow now.
King recognized explicitly that "injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere," which is in itself a succinct description of the inevitably shared nature of the climate calamity. But he also more precisely identified the most crucial "evils"--militarism, capitalism, and racism--that continue to enable accelerating climate chaos.
Few settings offer a more vivid depiction of how these intersections fuel climate change than corporate natural resource extraction. I referenced Dr. King's analysis of the "intersecting evils" at the Youth Climate Strike in San Jose in September 2019, using that example to explain the roles played by militarism, capitalism, and racism in the global climate catastrophe.

In addition to the climate crisis, another contemporary issue reflecting the intersections that MLK warned us to confront is police violence.
The role of racism is fairly obvious, if only in the remarkably consistent selection of which innocent people police kill with stunning [regularity](https://substack.com/redirect/36b2bf71-a8a9-4e61-92b6-6f7b12801e81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Militarism is also inevitably implicated: the surveillance tools and paramilitary combat gear to which police gain access are parts of the same military-industrial complex that [Eisenhower also warned us](https://substack.com/redirect/81885ea7-af57-43bf-9799-3f67af02bce9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to fear, while a disturbing number of police officers are recruiting from the ranks of veterans returning home from international conflict.
Capitalism also plays crucial roles in police violence with impunity. First, policing is a lucrative enterprise for everyone involved. Police officers--especially senior ones--are paid handsomely, despite increasingly visible reputations for responding to public safety crises like cowards. Beyond individual officers, public spending on police departments has grown as quickly as the costs of higher education and healthcare, while also crowding out other local budget items--many of which many be better poised to protect public safety--that have suffered as a result.
Finally, capitalism plays another, widely ignored role underlying policing, which San Francisco depicts painfully well. In our city, a backlash has emerged targeting unhoused people, overlooking their inherent dignity as human beings and the harsh reality that most of them were San Francisco residents who enjoyed stable housing until they didn't.
The casual disregard for other human beings that leads, for instance, a property owner to [douse](https://substack.com/redirect/f898133c-fc79-4eb4-82e7-b224f3437d41?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an unhoused person with water in the middle of winter, or an entitled tech worker to write [an open letter](https://substack.com/redirect/7d880e6f-0fd8-4994-ace2-531c817413c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) critical of their very existence, reflects the triumph of capitalism over humanity.
But this is not just an individual problem. Despite a judicial order prohibiting officials from doing so, and having had to [publicly apologize](https://substack.com/redirect/23279935-725d-4e5d-a8d4-19853a986ea0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for its prolific racism in the past, San Francisco has recently conducted [illegal sweeps of homeless encampments](https://substack.com/redirect/35f71a16-84d0-4b7a-ac96-b2db510d7c05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), seizing tents and lifesaving gear even in the midst of catastrophic storms and flooding.
The extent to which avarice has been institutionalized--from Washington, DC all the way down to the local level in cities across the U.S.--can not be overstated.
No one can say we were not warned.
### History offering hope--and caution
It's tough to reflect on how an ignorant civilization has watered down Dr. King's voice --despite his attaining the status of global justice icon, and losing his life to right wing terrorism--and feel any sense of hope for the future.
I'll offer a closing vignette for paid subscribers that might offer some hope, while admitting at the outset that the story also suggests its unfortunate limits. If nothing else, this coda--which helps explain how Martin Luther King, Jr. Day became a national holiday--helps indicate how artists and musicians can help inform history...
